Press Release

🔴 The Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine (PFLP) commends the Besharat Fatah operation against US bases and considers it a legitimate Iranian response to the US-zionist aggression.

- The Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine (PFLP) commends the Iranian operation "Besharat Fatah," which targeted US aggression bases in a legitimate response to the blatant US aggression against the Islamic Republic of Iran's nuclear facilities.

- As a sovereign state, Iran has the right to defend its land, its people, and its resources, and to respond to aggression by targeting US and zionist bases wherever they are located. This right is guaranteed by international law and the UN Charter.

- This response represents an advanced step in breaking US-zionist hegemony and deterrence in the region.

- The heavy "Besharat Fatah" missiles that struck US bases in the Gulf reflect a qualitative development in the deterrence equation and confirm that Iran possesses the will and capability to respond and will not allow the US-zionist enemy to determine the arena and terms of the confrontation.

- All American threats and intimidation over the past few days have failed to deter Tehran from exercising its sovereign right to respond. This should serve as an incentive for all the forces of the nation and the free people of the world to strengthen the confrontation front and enhance coordination among the resistance factions in the face of this dual American-zionist aggression.

- While we affirm our full support for the Islamic Republic of Iran in its just battle against American-zionist arrogance, we call on the people and their living forces to clearly express their rejection of the aggression and to stand with Iran and the resistance in the battle to defend sovereignty and dignity.

Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine
Central Media Department
June 23, 2025

Statement issued by the Islamic Jihad Movement in Palestine

The aggression carried out by the Trump administration against three Iranian nuclear sites under the supervision of the International Atomic Energy Agency is a blatant declaration of war against the brotherly Iranian people. It confirms that the US administration is the greatest enemy of the progress of the peoples of our region and our nation and their attainment of their legitimate rights through policies of hegemony and humiliation.

The actions of the Trump administration are stark evidence that the United States is the official sponsor of the zionist entity's terrorism and crimes against our Palestinian people and the peoples of our nation. The policies of this administration have become a danger to the region and the world, and even to the American people and their interests, serving the interests and delusions of the war criminal government in Tel Aviv.

We are fully confident that the brave and proud Iranian people, with their wise leadership and government, are capable of defending their legitimate rights, defending their land, and responding to the joint American and zionist aggression. We affirm our full support for all peoples facing occupation and aggression, foremost among them the dear Iranian people. We call upon the peoples of our nation to realize that the aggression against the Islamic Republic of Iran is a continuation of a plan to tighten its grip on the rest of the region's peoples and their capabilities, and to control their destinies.

Palestine Islamic Jihad Movement
Sunday, Dhu al-Hijjah 26, 1446 AH, 
June 22, 2025 AD

Press release issued by the Popular Resistance Committees in Palestine:

We condemn in the strongest terms the barbaric, treacherous American aggression against the Islamic Republic of Iran, which targeted the Fordow, Natanz, and Isfahan facilities. We consider this aggression a dangerous escalation and an aggression targeting the entire nation, calling for a unified stance from all free people of the nation to deter the arrogant aggressors.

The American aggression is an extension of the zionist aggression and crimes that pose a threat to the nation and its capabilities and target all those who refuse to submit, kneel, and surrender to the zionist project.

The American aggression proves that the American regime is the mother of all evils and wickedness in the world. It confirms the insistence of the criminal American administration and its reckless president on dragging the region into an open confrontation in service of and defense of the zionist terrorist and criminal entity, especially after the great failure suffered by the zionist entity in confronting the blessed strikes of the Iranian armed forces, which disrupted all its calculations, shook its foundations, and exposed its fragility, weakness, and inability to confront it alone.

The zionist-American aggression against the Islamic Republic of Iran will not break the resolve of the Iranian people, whose steadfastness, unity, patience, and strength have astonished all those harboring illusions about the ability to dismantle the rigid Iranian society. They have proven that they are a people firmly rooted in their land, proud and impervious to defeat.

We express our full solidarity with the Islamic Republic of Iran and its free and honorable people, and we affirm that this fateful, existential battle requires the unity of the nation's ranks, with all its components, to confront the zionist-American project aimed at subjugating the region and the entire nation.

Resistance Committees in Palestine.
Sunday, Dhu al-Hijjah 26, 1446 AH, corresponding to June 22, 2025 AD.

Message from the Public Relations of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) following the martyrdom of General Hajizadeh:

Following the aggression and the savage terrorist crime committed by the zionist regime on Friday, 13 Khordad 1404 HS, in an attack on one of the centers of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps in our beloved homeland, Brigadier General Amir Ali Hajizadeh - the brave and renowned commander of the IRGC Aerospace Force - was martyred, along with a group of courageous and loyal fighters from this force.

This diligent and striving general, who dedicated his blessed life - especially during his 16-year-long jihad and wise, influential leadership of the IRGC’s Aerospace Force - to strengthening the deterrent capability of the Islamic Republic and protecting the nation's security and independence, was one of the most prominent and influential figures in the Resistance Front, and one of the symbols of advancement, innovation, and strength in the fields of defense and missile capabilities in Islamic Iran. Through his spirit of jihad, honesty, high intelligence, humility, and devotion, he transformed this virtuous force into a main pillar of national deterrence and a thorn in the enemy’s side.

The martyrdom of this noble commander, alongside other martyred leaders and commanders of the armed forces and their devoted comrades, further reveals the terrorist nature of the zionist regime and its fear of the field commanders of the Islamic Revolution. Undoubtedly, this criminal act will not only fail to shake the firm path of the Iranian people and the IRGC but will also reinforce the determination for severe revenge and the continuation of the honorable path of the mujahideen in the cause of Allah.

While reaffirming the full security and operational control of the powerful IRGC and the other armed forces over the situation inside the country, we call on the noble Iranian nation to maintain calm, ignore the psychological manipulations of foreign enemies and their domestic agents, and to continue standing by the IRGC, as always, on the path of national unity, vigilance, and patience.

There is no doubt that the zionist entity and its sponsors in the region and beyond will receive the appropriate and decisive response at the right time and place. The path of dear Hajizadeh and his fellow martyrs - whose names will be remembered alongside historic anti-zionist operations like Sadiq-1 and Sadiq-2 in the history of this land - will continue with even greater strength than before.

Indeed, the oppressed and honorable martyrdom of this innovative, devoted, popular, and strategic commander for the Prophet (peace be upon him), for the Supreme Leader, for the command of Imam Khamenei, and for the Household of the Prophet (peace be upon him), will forever be remembered.

🇮🇷📸 | Pictures of Major General Martyr Amir Ali Hajizadeh

🟡 @NEWWORLDORDYR

🚨 Sanaa Salameh Daqqah is free after several days of detention and interrogation! 

The Haifa District Court decided today to release Sanaa Salameh Daqqah, the wife of the martyr prisoner and revolutionary thinker Walid Daqqah, under restrictive conditions, after accepting the appeal filed against the decision to extend her detention.

In a statement, attorney Fadi Bransi explained that the court was convinced there was no legal basis for Sanaa’s continued detention, affirming that her arrest was arbitrary and came as a result of her humanitarian demand for the release of her husband’s body, which is being held by the occupation.

Bransi added that Sanaa had committed no offense, and that the decision to release her represents an important legal step — but not the end of the struggle — stressing: “We are still demanding the release of Walid Daqqah’s body, and this is a human and familial right that cannot be relinquished.”

Today marks the passing anniversary of Dr. Ramadan Abdullah Shallah, former Secretary General of PIJ, who died of health issue on June 6, 2020. 

Dr. Ramadan Abdullah Shallah was born on January 1st, 1958, in al-Shujaiya, Gaza. In university, he met another great mind, Dr. Fathi Shiqaqi. After the martyrdom of Dr. Fathi Shiqaqi in 1995, he filled the leadership of the movement. Under his supervision, the expansion of the resistance in the West Bank happened, as well as the expansion of its alliances, especially with Hezbollah. In 2018, he was relieved of the position of Secretary-General of PIJ due to his illness and Commander Ziyad al-Nakhaleh took the position.

Hezbollah issued the following statement:

Hezbollah condemns the escalating barbaric American-"israeli" aggression against Syria, Yemen, Gaza, and Lebanon, which constitutes an extension of the open war waged by the American-Zionist axis of evil against the peoples of the region, destabilizing and undermining the security of their countries, violating their sovereignty, and depleting their capabilities and strengths, in order to subject them to the requirements of its hegemony and the interests of the Zionist entity, so that it has the upper hand in the region.

In light of this dangerous escalation, Hezbollah emphasizes the following:

• Targeting Syria through repeated raids and ongoing incursions into its territory falls within the framework of weakening the Syrian state and preventing it from recovering, and represents a blatant violation of its sovereignty.
• The heroic Resistance of the honorable people of Syria in the face of the Zionist incursion, which led to the fall of martyrs and wounded, is evidence that the choice of the Syrian people was and still is the choice of confrontation and Resistance against the occupier, and that the spirit of Resistance is rooted in the conscience of the Syrians.
• The continuation of the barbaric American aggression against Yemen, and committing massacres against its people, is a desperate attempt to dissuade the steadfast, proud Yemeni people from continuing to support Gaza and the Resistance in Palestine, and to push them to halt their heroic operations.
• Just as in Syria and Yemen, the escalating brutal "israeli" aggression against Palestine and Gaza, along with the ongoing genocide and displacement projects witnessed by a complacent international community, reveal the enemy's inability to break the will of Resistance and the spirit of steadfastness and defiance of the Palestinian people.
• In this context as well, the ongoing "israeli" assaults on Lebanon and the continuous American pressures—both through covering up these crimes and through envoys carrying "israeli" demands to impose on us—are part of the same aggressive strategy.
• This dangerous escalation places all the countries of the region and their peoples before historic responsibilities, compelling them to unite in confronting these perilous schemes that threaten everyone.
• The equation today is clear: either confrontation or surrender to the enemy's schemes, which seek nothing but to subjugate the region, bring it to its knees, and dominate its people and resources.

We, in Hezbollah, condemn these crimes and affirm our full solidarity with our sisterly Syria, beloved Yemen, and resilient Palestine, as well as their peoples. We call on all free people around the world to raise their voices against this unjust aggression and to pressure the international community to put an end to these repeated attacks, in light of this happening amid blatant American complicity that threatens regional peace and stability, opening the door to further escalation and aggressive wars in the region, all while the international community remains suspiciously silent.

‏السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكَ يَا شَهْرَ اللَّهِ الْأَكْبَرَ ، وَ يَا عِيدَ أَوْلِيَائِهِ .
السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكَ يَا أَكْرَمَ مَصْحُوبٍ مِنَ الْأَوْقَاتِ ، وَ يَا خَيْرَ شَهْرٍ فِي الْأَيَّامِ وَ السَّاعَاتِ .
السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكَ مِنْ شَهْرٍ قَرُبَتْ فِيهِ الْآمَالُ ، وَ نُشِرَتْ فِيهِ الْأَعْمَالُ .
السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكَ مِنْ قَرِينٍ جَلَّ قَدْرُهُ مَوْجُوداً ، وَ أَفْجَعَ فَقْدُهُ مَفْقُوداً ، وَ مَرْجُوٍّ آلَمَ فِرَاقُهُ .
السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكَ مِنْ أَلِيفٍ آنَسَ مُقْبِلًا فَسَرَّ ، وَ أَوْحَشَ مُنْقَضِياً فَمَضَّ .
السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكَ مِنْ مُجَاوِرٍ رَقَّتْ فِيهِ الْقُلُوبُ ، وَ قَلَّتْ فِيهِ الذُّنُوبُ .
السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكَ مِنْ نَاصِرٍ أَعَانَ عَلَى الشَّيْطَانِ ، وَ صَاحِبٍ سَهَّلَ سُبُلَ الْإِحْسَانِ .
السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكَ مَا أَكْثَرَ عُتَقَاءَ اللَّهِ فِيكَ ، وَ مَا أَسْعَدَ مَنْ رَعَى حُرْمَتَكَ بِكَ .
السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكَ مَا كَانَ أَمْحَاكَ لِلذُّنُوبِ ، وَ أَسْتَرَكَ لِأَنْوَاعِ الْعُيُوبِ .
السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكَ مَا كَانَ أَطْوَلَكَ عَلَى الْمُجْرِمِينَ ، وَ أَهْيَبَكَ فِي صُدُورِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ .
السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكَ مِنْ شَهْرٍ لَا تُنَافِسُهُ الْأَيَّامُ .
السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكَ مِنْ شَهْرٍ هُوَ مِنْ كُلِّ أَمْرٍ سَلَامٌ .
السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكَ غَيْرَ كَرِيهِ الْمُصَاحَبَةِ ، وَ لَا ذَمِيمِ الْمُلَابَسَةِ .
السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكَ كَمَا وَفَدْتَ عَلَيْنَا بِالْبَرَكَاتِ ، وَ غَسَلْتَ عَنَّا دَنَسَ الْخَطِيئَاتِ .
السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكَ غَيْرَ مُوَدَّعٍ بَرَماً وَ لَا مَتْرُوكٍ صِيَامُهُ سَأَماً .
السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكَ مِنْ مَطْلُوبٍ قَبْلَ وَقْتِهِ ، وَ مَحْزُونٍ عَلَيْهِ قَبْلَ فَوْتِهِ .
السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكَ كَمْ مِنْ سُوءٍ صُرِفَ بِكَ عَنَّا ، وَ كَمْ مِنْ خَيْرٍ أُفِيضَ بِكَ عَلَيْنَا .
السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكَ وَ عَلَى لَيْلَةِ الْقَدْرِ الَّتِي هِيَ خَيْرٌ مِنْ أَلْفِ شَهْرٍ .
السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكَ مَا كَانَ أَحْرَصَنَا بِالْأَمْسِ عَلَيْكَ ، وَ أَشَدَّ شَوْقَنَا غَداً إِلَيْكَ .
السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكَ وَ عَلَى فَضْلِكَ الَّذِي حُرِمْنَاهُ ، وَ عَلَى مَاضٍ مِنْ بَرَكَاتِكَ سُلِبْنَاهُ

🚨 Today, the occupation published footage of Dr. Hussam Abu Safiya shackled in prison. 

Dr. Abu Safiya was arrested on December 28, 2024, after occupation forces stormed Kamal Adwan Hospital. During the raid, he was severely beaten and used as a human shield. 

Abu Safiya lost his son who ascended to martyrdom due to occupation forces’ gunfire during the siege of Kamal Adwan Hospital. He was also injured by shrapnel from an IOF shell when occupation soldiers targeted the hospital, which was then destroyed and set on fire after being forcibly evacuating all medical staff and patients, some of whom were martyred. 

He was held in solitary confinement for 24 days.

Lawyer of Dr. Abu Safiya from Al-Mezan Center for Human Rights to Safa News Agency:
- Dr. Abu Safiya is suffering from severe health complications due to the torture he endured in occupation prisons.
- The torture he faced in Gaza before being transferred to the occupation’s prisons was brutal, involving inhumane methods that disregarded his status as a doctor.
- The charge against Dr. Abu Safiya is that he continued working at Kamal Adwan Hospital despite threats from the occupation army, in addition to working within the Gaza government affiliated with Hamas.
- These accusations do not classify him as an "unlawful combatant" and therefore do not strip him of his rights as a prisoner under the Fourth Geneva Convention.
- The Southern Command Chief of the occupation army issued a decision to detain Abu Safiya for 45 days until the "israeli" court upholds the arrest order.
- If the court confirms the "unlawful combatant" designation, Abu Safiya will be sentenced to six months of actual imprisonment, subject to renewal.
- The "unlawful combatant" status is similar to administrative detention in that the period can be extended. However, it differs in definition:
- An "unlawful combatant" is detained in war zones.
Administrative detention ends when the occupation deems the prisoner "no longer a threat," whereas an "unlawful combatant" remains in detention indefinitely.

Saturday's appointment with freedom for Amjad Taqatqa!

Amjad Taqatqa, from Beit Fajjar in Bethlehem, has been imprisoned since 2002. When he was arrested, his daughter Samah was a year and a half old and his younger daughter Anaheed 3 months old. He was subjected to 8 months of interrogation under torture, accused of recruiting and preparing the martyr Andaleeb Taqatqa, who carried out an operation in occupied Jerusalem, that killed 6 settlers and injured 85. During his imprisonment, he was subjected to isolation on multiple occasions.

Amjad Taqatqa will now be liberated by the Resistance in the Flood of the Free.

Tomorrow's appointment with freedom for Iyad Haribat!

Iyad Haribat, from Sakaka, near Dura, south of al-Khalil, has been imprisoned by the occupation since 2002 and sentenced to life imprisonment for participation in the Al-Aqsa Martyrs Brigades.

He has become one of the most prominent ill prisoners, subjected to medical neglect and mistreatment inside the occupation prisons. In 2014, Iyad was isolated for eight months and received an unidentified injection; after his isolation, he was unable to recognize his mother and brother. He lost an extreme amount of weight. In 2017 and again in 2021, he suffered massive health crises inside the prison. In 2017, this was caused by an attack by the occupation on the prisoners in Ramon prison,  and in 2021 he developed a septic infection; during that time, he received five surgeries. 

At the time, Munqidh Abu Atwan, of the Prisoners' Affairs Commission, said that Iyad had been subjected to five assassination attempts inside prison for his involvement in operations to avenge the martyrs Jamil Abu Atwan and Raed al-Karmi before his arrest.

Tomorrow, Iyad will finally be liberated, at the hands of the Resistance, in the Flood of the Free.

🇧🇭 Ayatollah Sheikh Isa Qassim on the occasion of the fourteenth anniversary of the Bahraini revolution [4/4]:

In Bahrain, there are honorable political prisoners who have been in prison for years, and they were only imprisoned for demanding justice and victory for the people.

Keeping these prisoners in prison for all this time, exhausting them, treating them harshly, depriving them of their religious freedom, and humiliating them greatly, all of this is a clear evidence of the incorrectness of the current policy and its hostility to freedom, the people, values, and the religion of Allah Almighty.

When (religious) rituals are fought inside the prison and in the public square of the people, what does this mean? This means hostility to religion... defying Allah Almighty... entering into a war against Allah Almighty.

What does it mean to torture this weak young man in prison and that old man in prison, what does this mean? It means lack of mercy, it means dryness of heart, it means cruelty of heart, it means mockery of humanity, all of this is lived inside our beloved Bahrain.

It is a disgrace to the people, and the people are responsible before Allah Almighty for every moment they remain unjustly imprisoned in their prisons. If the people could save their brothers and sons from their prisons, they would have to save them at this moment, not to mention the next moment.

This situation cannot provide stability even if all the problems are solved, the problems of the people living outside the prison, and the prisoners remain. It is also impossible to stabilize the security situation, the political situation inside Bahrain, as it is like in any other country.

If the government is thinking about political stability, it must solve the problems.. the problems of the people that it caused. However, if it is looking for political stability by other than solving these problems, it is delusional and the people will not die.

The people, like governments, may weaken but remain weak, and just as governments can grow stronger after weakness, so can the people grow stronger after weakness. The conditions conducive to the policy of injustice may change, and the day that the conditions that support injustice change and the earthly gods fall, and the day that the earthly gods that any government depends on fall, then the government's regret, retreat, or mercy will be of no use, as the matter will be otherwise, just as the people are tormented, the governments are tormented if the equation of power is reversed if the governments insist on the path of injustice until they become weak. 

The only way that the differences between the people and the governments must end is either for the governments to respond to the demands of the people or for the people to become strong and emerge from their weakness, and emerging from weakness, as it is an open path for governments, is also an open path for the people. It is not impossible for any people to seek the path of seeking power and reach it, and the word of the strong people when they are strong is not their word when they are weak, and there is no motive or obstacle for governments and no opportunity for them to enslave the people except while the people remain weak. 

It is the responsibility of the people to seek power, to seek power in all areas of power to save themselves from a life of humiliation, disgrace, and great troubles.

🇧🇭 Ayatollah Sheikh Isa Qassim on the occasion of the fourteenth anniversary of the Bahraini revolution [3/4]:

Bahrain and other countries cannot emerge from their crises as long as politics in them oppose the people, oppose them in their religion, oppose them in their dignity, oppose them in their freedom, seek to corrupt them, and thwart any movement on their part to reform their situation.

Security, calm, stability and a positive relationship between any government and its people necessarily require a position from the government that serves the people, not for the people to become servants of the government, of the government’s individuals, of the ruling entity that exists in a way that does not care about the people’s religion, their livelihood or their security, but rather seeks against all of that.

No government can by force, comes and goes, decreases and increases, and in all its circumstances it cannot eliminate the will of the honorable person, and there is no one more honorable than a person who embraces the true religion of Allah, and there is no way to raise the level of dignity and pride in a person and to push his will on the path of steadfastness, sublimity and elevation as can happen under Islam.

In Bahrain or any other country, where there is a Christian and a Muslim, or a Sunni and a Shiite, the Christian is mistaken and the Muslim is mistaken when he sees that one of them is defeating the other by joining the unjust policy.

The Sunni in Bahrain is completely wrong, and the Shiite in Bahrain is completely wrong when this or that sees that it is in his interest to weaken the other party so that the word of unjust politics is strengthened, and so that unjust politics can continue on the path of injustice and continue to weaken the people.

🇧🇭 Ayatollah Sheikh Isa Qassim on the occasion of the fourteenth anniversary of the Bahraini revolution [2/4]:

When an Islamic country, a political system joins the normalization led by America; the meaning of that is suffering for the nation, for the people and for the nation, and the downfall of their value and the exhaustion of their existence, and it is a path in which there is the destruction of any glorious nation, any upright religion and any dignity on earth.

When the problems that have moved people to demand their rights increase, then it is impossible for there to be a retreat from demanding rights, and retreating from demanding rights means nothing but surrendering to death, surrendering to humiliation, accepting downfall, accepting deviation and humiliation and entering into slavery to others.

It is impossible for this nation - including the people of Bahrain - to retreat as long as Islam pulsates in their veins, as long as Islam exists in their hearts, as long as they have some remembrance of Allah Almighty.

The requirement of the movement remains, so the movement must remain, the obstacle to the success of the movement or even the origin of the movement disrupts it or disrupts its effectiveness but does not deprive it of its requirement. The requirement does not deprive its requirement of a strong obstacle of any great degree.

Islam does not accept for its nation, neither for the individual of the nation nor for the nation nor for the people of the nation, to give up the right in a way that would lose rights and rights would fall and falsehood would replace the right. 

Any concession of a right that allows falsehood to continue, to be rampant, and to rule is a forbidden concession and a concession that Islam does not accept. If people complain of fatigue, of the fatigue of continuing on the path of the demand and on the path of the movement, and their souls tell them to retreat, then Islam says to its followers, whoever has truly converted to Islam, you do not have the right to retreat. 

Silence on the demands that affect, which are the core of Islam, silence on the demand for dignity, freedom, and the fulfillment of the rights that give man the ability to play the divine role that man exercises on earth, silence on these matters is a betrayal of Islam, a disruption of Islam.

🇧🇭 Ayatollah Sheikh Isa Qassim on the occasion of the fourteenth anniversary of the Bahraini revolution [1/4]:

There was a need behind the “February 14” movement. It did not move out of nowhere. The people did not move out of nowhere. The movement was not based on an emotional motive, nor a love of fatigue. The problem of throwing oneself into the arms of zionism and entering into a broad, satanic alliance that is by nature hostile to the nation... hostile to religion... destroying the humanity of man... corrupting the earth, this means a problem above all problems, a problem that leaves no honor for any honorable people, no sanctity for religion, and no dignity for man.

🇧🇭 National opposition forces in Bahrain: “We trust in Allah” and we bet on our dear people to continue the revolution until decades of tyranny and dependency are ended.

In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful

On the occasion of the glorious anniversary of the February 14 Revolution, the national opposition forces in Bahrain record all pride and honor for this great people who were not defeated by the challenges and calamities witnessed by our country and the countries of the region, and refused to compromise or retreat, and all forms of oppression and repression did not succeed in forcing them to sign the instrument of surrender and slavery, and the outcome of these years was more steadfastness, defiance and continuation of the popular movement in order to achieve freedom and defend the identity of the people and their sanctities and end tyranny and dependence on the outside.

In light of this glorious anniversary, we in the national opposition forces emphasize the following points:

1- The reasons that accumulated crises in our country over the past decades, and ultimately led to the outbreak of the February 14, 2011 Revolution, have not ended or changed, but rather increased in intensity; As a result of the intransigence of the authority and its violations, regional and international complications were added that doubled it, while it was supposed to force the authority to stop its tyranny and its neglect of the sovereignty of the people and the homeland.

2- We express our sincere appreciation and gratitude to our people for their patience and steadfastness during the past years, as they were able to draw the equation of truth and steadfastness in the face of the brutality of the authority, which means that their strength stems from their belief in their values ​​and noble goals, and it is so steadfast and authentic that oppression, arrest, displacement and systematic deprivation cannot subjugate it and break its will, and this steadfastness that was evident in 2011 is still present today, and with more vitality, which confirms that it will be the successful bet to achieve victory, God willing.

▪️ Israeli occupation arrests at least 380 Palestinians in northern occupied West Bank amid ongoing aggression
Palestinian Prisoners' Society (PPS)

13/2/2025
Ramallah, Palestine – At least 380 Palestinians have been subject to arrest and detention by Israeli occupation soldiers across the northern West Bank governorates of Jenin, Tulkarem and Tubas, amid Israel’s ongoing massive assault on those areas, the Palestinian Prisoner’s Society (PPS) said in a statement today. 

In Jenin, at least 150 Palestinians were subject to arrest and detention in Jenin and its refugee camp (the highest) during the past 24 days of the occupation’s aggression. In the Tulkarem governorate - as of the 18th day of the occupation’s attack - the number of arrests has reached at least 125. In Tubas, which underwent a 17-day assault, at least 100 arrests have been recorded.

These figures include those who were arrested and remain in the occupation’s custody, as well as those who were later released. It does not include the dozens of Palestinians who are subject to hours-long field interrogations on a near-nightly basis, which are accompanied by severe beatings, destructive military raids on homes, and systematic abuse and humiliation of the detainees and their families. These raids constitute organized state-terror and have severe, lasting effects on Palestinian families. 

The arrests targeted all segments of Palestinian society, including children, women, young people, the wounded, and the elderly. These mass arrest campaigns are an extension of the occupation’s decades-long systematic policies of persecuting the Palestinian population. Such policies have escalated in an unprecedented manner following the genocide and the rise of atrocities committed against Palestinian detainees.

THE FLOOD OF THE FREE: The march to liberation

This morning, as the sun rises in Gaza, as the people of the north return home without anyone searching or interrogating them, in preparation for a greater march home to all of occupied Palestine and their original homes and lands, as Jabalia camp is filled with its people and families, from the heroic resistance fighters that made the camp a death trap for the genocidal enemy until its last moment, as the doctors, nurses and health workers reopen the hospitals, as the Palestinian police, interior ministry and civil defense — so heavily targeted by the occupier — fan out throughout Gaza, Palestinian prisoners inside the occupation jails are awaiting their liberation in the exchange agreement, Toufan al-Ahrar, the Flood of the Free — liberated by the Palestinian resistance and the great sacrifices of Gaza, accompanied by those throughout Palestine, of Lebanon, Yemen, Iran and Iraq.

The occupation has released a list of names of the prisoners to be liberated within the first stage of the agreement, a process that will take place over 42 days. This is not the entire reach of the Flood of the Free; especially as many of the leadership prisoners and those with high sentences are expected to be demanded to be released in the second stage of the exchange, in return for high-ranking Zionist captives held by the resistance. 

The names on the list published by the occupation are exciting and inspiring, including so many of the great leaders of the Palestinian liberation struggle; three of the heroes of the Freedom Tunnel; Nael Barghouthi, the longest-held Palestinian prisoner; Mohammed Abu Warda, with 45 life sentences; Khalida Jarrar, the leading Palestinian academic and leftist; Ammar al-Zaben, the first Palestinian prisoner to conceive a child through "smuggled sperm;" Dirar al-Sisi, the Palestinian engineer kidnapped from Ukraine in 2011; Hadeel Shatara, the Palestinian educator and activist;  Iyad Jaradat, who endured years in solitary confinement; Wael Jaghoub, the Palestinian leader and writer behind bars; Bushra al-Taweel, the tireless chronicler of the Palestinian prisoners' cause; the ill strugglers like Moatassem Raddad and Mansour Muqtada; Abla Sa'adat, the wife of Ahmad Sa'adat — and so many more. 

However, we also know that the exchange will be completed in stages and throughout the 42 days, and that the Resistance — the great Resistance that achieved their liberation — is the only trustworthy source of information, and will be coordinating the release of the lists of names of prisoners to be exchanged each day of the releases. 

We will provide immediate translations and information as soon as they are available on an ongoing basis. 

1,737 male and female prisoners will be released from Israeli occupation prisons by the Resistance as part of the FIRST PHASE ONLY of the Flood of the Free. 

We urge everyone, around the world, to join with the Palestinian people in Gaza and everywhere, to celebrate the achievements of the Resistance and the humiliation of the occupier, and to celebrate and welcome each liberated prisoner as the heroes of our global movement that they are, in public events, with posters and public education, with actions and events exposing Zionism and imperialism everywhere. Receive the prisoners virtually and symbolically — and pledge to continue the movement until all of them are free, and all of Palestine is free, from the river to the sea.

🚨 Important statement issued by the Prisoners Media Office:
—
Mechanism for the Release of Prisoners in the First Phase of the Exchange Deal

According to Riyad Al-Ashqar, Director of the Palestine Center for Prisoners Studies:

Gradual Release, Not All at Once:
- The prisoners will not be released in a single wave. 
- Although the occupation has published lists of their names on the Prison Authority's website (a routine procedure to allow for objections), the process will not occur simultaneously.

Implementation in Several Batches:
- The release will take place over 4-6 weeks.
- Each week, a new batch of prisoners will be freed.
The full number will be completed within 42 days, unless the duration is shortened through efforts by mediators.

Negotiations Are Ongoing:
- The second phase of the deal will be negotiated during this period.
- It will involve identifying new names and numbers of detainees to be released in exchange for occupation captives.

🚨 Palestinian Prisoners Center: Details of the First Phase of the Prisoner Exchange Deal

The first phase of the prisoner exchange deal agreed upon between the resistance in Gaza and the occupation includes the release of more than half of the prisoners sentenced to life imprisonment.

Specifics of the First Phase:
- The first phase will include the release of 600 prisoners, all of whom have sentences exceeding 15 years.
- Among them, approximately 290 prisoners are serving life sentences, representing about half of the 566 prisoners sentenced to life in occupation prisons.

Veteran Prisoners:
- The release will include 21 veteran prisoners detained before the Oslo Accords, who have spent decades in occupation prisons or serving life sentences. 
- The shortest-serving among them has completed 30 consecutive years of imprisonment.
- These prisoners will have priority for release in the deal.

Freed Prisoners Re-Arrested:
- The first phase will also include 47 prisoners released in the 2011 Wafaa Al-Ahrar Deal but re-arrested by the occupation.
- Among them are veteran prisoners who had served decades behind bars before their previous release.
- Many were sentenced again to life imprisonment.

Women, Children, and Vulnerable Groups:
- The first phase will secure the release of all 85 female prisoners from across all regions.
- It will include all minors, numbering over 340 children.
- A number of elderly prisoners and those suffering from severe illnesses will also be released.

Prisoners from Gaza, the West Bank, Al-Quds, and Palestinian lands occupied in 1948:
- The phase will include prisoners with high sentences from all regions, including Gaza, the West Bank, Al-Quds, and inside 1948-occupied lands.
- Additionally, 1,000 prisoners from Gaza arrested during the genocide war on the strip will be released, out of a total of more than 3,000 prisoners still detained from Gaza.

The Importance of the Deal:

- The deal is significant not only for the large numbers of prisoners to be released but also for the caliber of prisoners included.

Priority will be given to approximately 1,500 prisoners, including:
- Veteran prisoners and those with high sentences who have spent many years behind bars.
- 539 "deans of prisoners" who have served over 20 consecutive years in prison.
- 43 prisoners who have been detained for more than 25 years.
- 21 prisoners held for over 30 years, including the oldest prisoner, Mohammed Al-Tous from Al-Khalil, who has been imprisoned since October 1985.

A Historic Exchange:
- The deal, if completed as planned, will be the most significant prisoner exchange in history, executed by the Palestinian resistance despite the occupation’s opposition.
- It will include prisoners who were previously impossible to release in earlier deals nor through the goodwill agreements facilitated by the Palestinian Authority in 2013.

Ramallah - The Prisoners' and Freed Prisoners' Affairs Authority and the Palestinian Prisoners' Club announced the martyrdom of four detainees from Gaza, one day after the announcement of the martyr Ashraf Abu Warda. The martyrs are: Mohammed Rashid Akka (44 years old), Samir Mahmoud Al-Kahlout (52 years old), Zuhair Omar Al-Sharif (58 years old), and Mohammed Anwar Labad (57 years old), bringing the number of martyrs announced during the past 24 hours to five martyrs.

The Prisonersx Authority and The Prisoners' Club noted that we received the news of Akka's martyrdom through the General Authority for Civil Affairs, while the family of the martyr Al-Kahlout received the news of his martyrdom through the Hamoked Foundation, the martyr Al-Sharif through a private lawyer who obtained a paper proving his martyrdom in Ramla Prison, and the martyr Labad through the Hamoked Foundation. 

The Prisoners' Authority and The Prisoners' Club stated that all of these responses received by the various institutions were obtained from the occupation army or the occupation prison administration, and we confirm that this is the only available way to reveal the fate of the Gaza detainees that was made possible by the legal amendments. 

The Prisoners' Authority and The Prisoners' Club explained that the martyr Akka was arrested on 11/15/2023 during his displacement from northern Gaza to the south. He's married and the father of ten children, and according to his family, he did not suffer from any health problems. He was ascended to martyrdom in the Negev Prison yesterday. 

As for the martyr Al-Kahlout, he was arrested on 10/25/2024, from Kamal Adwan Hospital. Before his arrest, he had undergone an operation to remove parts of his liver and kidneys, and he needed intensive care. His health condition worsened due to the simple medical capabilities. Despite the doctors’ attempts to prevent the soldiers from arresting him, they arrested him by force, and he passed away on 11/3/2024, a week after his arrest. It is worth noting that he's married and the father of three children. 

The martyr Al-Sharif has been detained since 10/7/2023, as he was arrested while working in the occupied territories in 1948. He's married and has six children. According to his family, he did not suffer from any health problems. He was martyred on 10/18/2023. Here, we point out that the occupation army is trying to manipulate the responses that followed the lawyer’s response, despite obtaining a paper confirming his martyrdom in Ramla prison. The process of manipulating the responses was represented by the army giving us another response stating that he had been released from Ofer prison, and another response stating that there were no authorities to give a response regarding his fate. This case is one of several cases that the institutions followed up on, which showed the occupation army’s deliberate manipulation of the responses.

The martyr Labad was arrested on 11/18/2024, during his displacement from the north to the south with his family. He is married and has eight children. Before his arrest, he suffered from liver cirrhosis and diabetes. He was ascended to martyrdom on 11/27/2024. 

The Prisoners' Authority and the Prisoners' Club said that the increase in the number of martyred prisoners is an escalating humanitarian disaster, and a new confirmation of what we warned about, that the occupation, without the slightest regard for all of humanity, is working to liquidate prisoners in a systematic and public manner.

Statement to the Palestinian people regarding the practices of the Authority in Jenin.

⚫️ Issued by the Islamic Jihad Movement in Palestine:

Based on the firm positions and solid principles upon which the Islamic Jihad Movement in Palestine was founded, and which it has adhered to throughout its journey, and which everyone near and far testifies that it has never deviated from,

In light of the unjust campaign targeting our movement, its cadres and symbols, with the aim of provoking it and dragging it into a cycle of rejected internal fighting,
And based on our deep concern to abide by the will of our Palestinian people, as the owner of legitimacy and the final decision, we present to Palestinian public opinion the following facts:

Since the launch of the security campaign carried out by the Authority's security forces in the Jenin camp, our movement has been subjected to a systematic media campaign aimed at distorting its path, casting doubt on its national affiliation, and denying its honorable history and great sacrifices. This campaign has reached the level of personal attacks on the movement's leaders and symbols. Despite all this, we affirm our adherence to refusing to be dragged into internal strife, not out of weakness or inability, but rather out of commitment to our principles, to preserve the blood of our people, and in appreciation of the sacrifices of the martyrs, wounded, and prisoners. We are fully aware that these provocations aim to drag us into the quagmire of strife, which we will not enter.

Our brothers and sons in the Jenin Brigade have also been subjected, for weeks, to a fierce campaign of arrests, pursuits, and shooting, amidst a media and security siege aimed at undermining their resolve. These practices aim to push us into internal conflict, which only serves the zionist occupation that incites strife, aiming to portray our Palestinian people as being in a state of internal fighting, to pass its narrative and justify its crimes against Gaza before the world.

From the first moment, we took the initiative to put forward numerous initiatives and mediations with the aim of sparing our people the scourge of strife and stopping the bloodshed. Despite our keenness to prevail wisdom, all these initiatives were thwarted, and the doors were closed to all peaceful solutions.

Events have taken a dangerous turn with the escalation of campaigns of repression and abuse practiced by the authorities against citizens who express their opinions rejecting sedition. These practices, which aim to silence voices calling for wisdom and reason, constitute a flagrant violation of freedom of expression and contradict the sanctity of Palestinian blood.

Based on the above, we emphasize the following:
1 - We demand that the Palestinian Authority immediately stop its security and media campaigns, lift the siege on Jenin camp, end the persecution of resistance fighters, and stop the abuse of citizens.
2 - We commend the positions of our brothers in the Palestinian forces and factions who presented initiatives to lift the siege on Jenin and the efforts and endeavors they made to quell the strife and stop the bloodshed of our people, and to thwart the occupation, and we call on them to redouble their efforts.
3 - We call on our brothers in the Fatah movement, with whom we share the path of struggle and resistance, to intervene urgently to stop the voices that insult their honorable history of struggle, and to redirect the compass towards our national unity.
4 - We appeal to the dignitaries and national leaders, from various political and societal movements, to play their role in protecting Palestinian blood and working to find a way out that preserves the unity of our people.
As we address our people, we renew our firm commitment to preserving our national unity and our absolute rejection of all forms of sedition that serve the occupation alone.

The first Lebanese figure to visit Syria since Assad's fall:

Walid Jumblatt, Druze leader,former Lebanese MP and former Progressive Socialist Party (PSP) leader, met with Hayat Tahrir al-Sham’s (HTS) Ahmad al-Sharaa (Abu Muhammad al-Julani) on 22 December, after arriving in the Syrian capital Damascus at the head of a political and religious delegation.
 
During the meeting, Jumblatt offered his “salute” to the Syrian people in their “battle” which ended the “oppression and tyranny” of the former government of Bashar al-Assad. 

Sharaa, in turn, said while meeting with Jumblatt that "Syria was a source of concern and disturbance and its interference in Lebanese affairs was negative, and the Syrian regime killed Kamal Jumblatt, Bashir Gemayel and Rafik Hariri.” 

"The coming Syria in the new era stands at an equal distance from everyone in Lebanon and will not be a case of negative interference in Lebanon at all,” Sharaa vowed. 

HTS, formerly known as Al-Qaeda’s Nusra Front, was responsible for the killing and kidnapping of Lebanese army soldiers during the first years of the war in Syria. It also occupied large swathes of the barrens of Arsal and Ras Baalbek in northern Lebanon, and carried out deadly attacks on Lebanese civilians. 

Prior to the formation of the Nusra Front in 2012, Julani was a member of the Islamic State of Iraq (ISI), the group which evolved into ISIS – also responsible for numerous atrocities in Lebanon.

📌 "Israels" Northern Community Reels from Economic Paralysis 
Al-Akhbar report 
 
The war’s toll on Israel’s northern regions has become a staggering economic burden. With damages exceeding 5 billion shekels ($1.35 billion), the Israeli Tax Authority warns that the figure will only climb without a ceasefire agreement with Lebanon. Direct damage to homes, factories, and infrastructure totals 1.5 billion shekels, while indirect losses—shuttered businesses, halted agriculture, and declining incomes—reach 4 billion shekels, with agriculture and tourism hardest hit. 
 
Factories near the border are closed, and major shipping disruptions have choked supply chains, crippling production. Even in areas farther from the frontlines, workforces have dwindled, operating at barely 50% capacity as missile strikes and sirens disrupt daily life. Over 27 settlements have ceased operations entirely, following Hezbollah's evacuation warnings three weeks ago. 
 
In a blow to Israel's defense industry, precision strikes by Hezbollah drones forced the closure of key military factories, including facilities belonging to "B.A.T" and "Rafael". These attacks have led to layoffs, factory relocations, and the evacuation of sensitive military sites. The once-bustling real estate market has collapsed, with plummeting demand and over 12,000 families displaced, many now facing up to 18 months in temporary housing.  
 
Interviews with Israeli business owners impacted in the northern regions under occupation emphasized their recognition of the "need for joint cooperation with the Arab community" in these areas. These regions are home to over two million people, including approximately 850,000 Arabs who form a vital part of the business and production sectors and a significant consumer base. 
 
The war’s shadow continues to freeze reconstruction, deepen unemployment, and strain resources, threatening to lock the northern economy in paralysis for the foreseeable future.

لا بُدَّ من بعدَ مرورِ شهرين على هذه الحرب الإلتفات إلى عدّة نقاط مازالت عالقة عند كثيرٍ منّا حتّى اليوم،،

النقطة الأولى وهي مسألة أنَّ المقاومةَ بخير، وهذا المبدأ لا يتنافى مع وجود التضحيات الجِسام التي تُقدّمها المقاومة بشكلٍ يوميّ إلى جانبِ تضحيات أبناءِ شعبها، فعندما تكونُ المُقاومةُ بخير يعني أنّها تُقف أمام التّحديّات والصّعوبات وأن تقف سدًا منيعًا أمام طموحات العدوّ وأهدافه وتمنعهُ من تحقيقها بشكلٍ مستدام.
هذا الأمر قد يتصوّرهُ البعض نتيجةً لمُغالطَات زُرِعت منذُ أكثر من عشرة أعوام  نتيجةً لكثير من التُرَّهات الإعلاميّة، هو أنَّ مسألة صمود المقاومة مرتبطة بحزب التضحيات، فيما المسألة بالعكس تمامًا، فإنَّ المُقاومة تكونُ بخير عندما تكون تعمل وتسعى لتحقيق أهدافها ويكفي وجودها وبقاءَها دليلٌ على ذلك.  

النقطة الثانية وهي نقطةُ النظرة إلى جيش العدوّ، وهذه من المُعضلات التي عشناها لمدة سنوات كذلك نتيجةً لبعض السّخافات الإعلامّة التي كانت موجودة في أوساطنا والتي تبنّاها الكثير منّا مع الأسف، فإذا كانَ الجيش الإسرائيلي جيشًا هزيلًا فهذا يعني أنّهُ لا قيمة لنفس هذه المُقاومة والإعجاز الذي حققتهُ بكونها مقاومةً موجودةً صامدةً أمام هذا الجيش يعني أنّ هذا الجيش هو جيشٌ متين وهو بالأصل كذلك فهو يمتلك التفوّق التّقني والتكنولوجي والتّسليحي والمدد غير  المحدود من العالمِ كلّه، وصولًا إلى بعض الأنظمةِ العربيةِ السخيفة التي لا تمتلك حتى قرارها. 

المسألة الثّالثة والأهم وهي مسألةُ اليقين التّام بأنّ النّصر يتحقق بتحقُق أهداف المُقاومة، فلا يُقنعنّكُم أحدٌ بأن هذا النّصر يوم يتحقق سيكون عكس ذلك، هذه المُقاومة وهذه القيادة أمينة على التضحيات التي قُدّمت وهي التضحيات الجسامُ العظامُ الكبار.
إنّ قيادة المقاومة أمينة على هذه التضحيات وتعرف كيف وأين وعلى ماذا تُفاوض، وكيف يمكن أن يكون النّسق الذي سيخرج فيه الإتّفاق حين خروجه، وإلى ذلك الحين نسألُ الله الثّبات لأُمّتنا وشعبنا العزيز وللبيئةِ الشريفة المُضحية التي ما تركت المقاومة وما وخذلتها، بل وحتّى كانت هي السّند والحُصُن وغمد السّيف حتى في أصعب الظروف.

🚨 The Situation of the Female Prisoners as reported by "Qadeyah" and translated by RNN:
—
Since the beginning of the aggression, the female prisoners have endured compounded suffering due to the escalation of abuse and the denial of their most basic rights by the prison administration. This is in addition to the exploitation of their cases by some lawyers who have profited from them, whether by taking on their representation or facilitating visits.

The current situation summarized:

- One change of clothing per prisoner: The prison administration bargained with the prisoners by offering them a single winter outfit in exchange for confiscating their summer clothing. The outfit is of poor-quality fabric, shrinking with washing and fraying easily. Even a "half needle"—a broken needle the prisoners used to mend their clothing—was confiscated during one of last month's raids.

- Scarce and poor-quality food: The administration continues to provide food that is both insufficient in quantity and substandard in quality, leading to issues such as constipation and hormonal imbalances among the prisoners.

- Sick prisoners without proper treatment: The unbearable conditions of imprisonment for a healthy detainee are doubly harsh for those suffering from illnesses. There is no doctor to treat them nor appropriate medication. The jailers deliberately replace proper treatment with a single "Acamol" pill or sedative injections.

- Punishment for the simplest acts: The prison administration punishes prisoners for minor actions such as delays in using the bathroom or even for laughing together as they try to lift their spirits through conversation or games. Punishments include being denied outdoor time for days, cell raids, strip searches, beatings, and confiscation of the few possessions they have left in their rooms.

- Deprived of cleaning supplies: There is no laundry detergent available, only dish soap. They have had no nail clippers for nearly a month, and only one comb is available in the entire section. Some prisoners are now suffering from fungal infections on their skin and scalp due to these conditions.

- Exploitation by lawyers: Since the beginning of the aggression and the suspension of family visits, some lawyers have exploited families by charging exorbitant amounts to facilitate visits with the prisoners. This is compounded by the poor treatment of some lawyers and their failure to deliver even simple messages between the prisoners and their families.

The suffering is indescribable, and the conditions are unbearable. Yet, the prisoners, as one witness says, "are incredibly strong." They draw their strength from their closeness to Allah, filling their days with prayer, recitation of the Quran, and fasting. They remain resilient, but this does not absolve every one of their duty to advocate for and support them.

Behind these walls, our women have been stripped of their freedom.
In the midst of silence and oppression, Palestinian female prisoners bear their suffering and pave the way for our freedom

From within the depths of prison, Palestinian women’s stories of strength and resilience unfold amidst their endurance of the harshest forms of torture and deprivation. 

Among them wounded women, children, and mothers deprived of their children. Palestinian prisoners endure unbearable suffering from the moment they are detained until their very last moment in prison. The suffering isn’t confined within the occupation’s prisons; on the contrary, the effects of the excruciating physical and mental torture remain with the freed prisoners long after their release.

Consistent violations of their freedoms and sanctities, as the occupation forcibly removes the prisoners’ hijabs.
Any attempt to invoke their right to refuse the removal of their hijab is met with barbarity and further forced naked searches. Occupation forces have not only confiscated Palestinian prisoners’ hijabs, but they have also confiscated any needles or threads to prevent prisoners from repairing the torn shoes and clothing that the occupation had replaced their modest attire with. Prisoners have no choice but to borrow each others’ shoes when they need them to go outside for breaks or to meet with their lawyers.

Systematic medical neglect; the necessary medical care is not provided to any of the women inside the occupation’s prisons facing diseases, injuries, and mental ailments exacerbated inside the prison.
The hardest times, reported by many imprisoned women, are during pregnancy, where they are denied all necessary medical attention and even deprived of sunlight.

Prison cells unfit for human habitation. Humidity, moldy walls, and blankets—if provided— that reek. This is how prisoners describe their rooms in which they are held.
Torture is meticulously designed into every detail possible, where, in addition to the widespread insects and pests that spread diseases and infections, the lights are turned on and off at the whim of the occupation soldiers and guards, after cutting access to control them from inside the cells. 

A Palestinian prisoner describes her arrest: “My house was raided and I was detained by special forces a week after the start of the assault on Gaza. I was taken to a police station in my residential area while being threatened with execution or “deportation to Gaza” along with insults and intimidation tactics.” She was then transferred, her hands and feet shackled, to an isolated area, where she was subjected to shabah (a form of torture) under heavy rain alongside prisoners from Gaza, in a brutal and horrifying scene.

Talking about the first hours inside Al-Damon prison, she says: “When I first entered, campaigns of repression and cell raids using batons and tear gas began, and all essential supplies that were previously available to prisoners including cooking utensils, electrical appliances, books, and more, were confiscated.” Based on what she was told by other prisoners, she added “Women are being detained in unprecedented numbers from across Palestine on a daily basis and at an increasingly rapid rate.”

She also mentioned the harsh circumstances and constant threats of rape that women from Gaza are experiencing. Many had been abducted from shelters and know nothing about their children or their whereabouts. One Palestinian prisoner from Gaza says, “I was abducted and I know nothing about my four-month-old baby, my heart is shattering with worry over him.”

🚨 BREAKING: A French court approved the release of Georges Abdallah, the longest-held political prisoner in Europe—the internationalist, communist, fighter for liberation.

He is set to be freed on December 6th, 2024, after recently reaching 40 years of imprisonment. His release is contingent upon him leaving France.

For four decades years, immense popular pressure has been directed on French, American, and European authorities to secure his freedom. Last summer, Georges submitted the ninth request for his release, as his minimum sentence ended in 1999. Despite this, American intervention repeatedly blocked his release. Notably, his eighth release request, which was approved by the French judiciary in 2013, would have seen his release, but was overturned by American pressure, absurdly arguing that that the 72-year-old man "may return to the battlefield." It was revealed in 2015 that Hillary Clinton ordered French FM Fabius to keep Georges imprisoned in 2015. In fact, Georges has been eligible for release since 1999, but the state fears his capacity to galvanize the masses.

Georges, from his humble Lebanese village of Al-Qoubaiyat, he was inspired by the revolutionary broadcasts of the PFLP, and soon formed and led the Lebanese Armed Revolutionary Factions (LARF). When the zionist enemy invaded Lebanon in 1982, he worked tirelessly to halt the supply of weapons from Europe to the zionist entity. He was arrested in 1984 under fabricated charges—French police claimed that weapons that were never present were found. By 1987, he was convicted for the alleged murder of a US Army officer and a zionist embassy official in Paris.

Georges neither confirmed nor denied the allegations. To him, the trial was a farce. His position was clear: the French state and its authorities are complicit in the occupation of Lebanon and Palestine. To him, the courts were theatrics, a tool of imperialism to obfuscate the complicity of the US and France in the bombing of his country. 

In his own words:
"Comrades, let us be vigilant and prevent the zionist criminals from annihilating the revolutionary heroes in captivity!"
"Let us be worthy of the epic of the torches of freedom, those invincible resistance heroes detained in zionist prisons! Naturally, the popular Palestinian masses and their fighting vanguards in captivity can count on your active solidarity. May a thousand solidarity initiatives flourish for the sake of Palestine and its glorious resistance."
"Glory and eternity to the martyrs! Victory to the masses and the struggling peoples."
"Yes to building a comprehensive Arab resistance front to confront normalization in all forms!"
"Down with imperialism and its agents, the zionists, and Arab reactionaries!"
"Together, Comrades, and only together will we win!"

Let us heed his words, remaining vigilant and committed to his vision for justice and liberation, for Georges and for all prisoners in zionist, western, and Arab reactionary prisons.

The sister of the 25-year-old Hezbollah martyr Hassan Al-Askari Ali Tahmaz "Dima Ali", who was ascended on October 13, 2024:

"We received the news of my brother's martyrdom just minutes before the Maghrib prayer. My mother wept, then said, 'Indeed, to Allah we belong, and to Him we shall return,' and at the first sound of the call to prayer, she went directly to pray.

She had an exam that day, as she's among those who have memorized the Holy Qur’an, so she continued to memorize it quietly in the next room with all strength and faith.

I heard my father say, 'No condolences, congratulate me,' as he took the first call from relatives, thanking Allah for the ascension of his eldest son, the apple of his eye, as a martyr on road to Al-Quds, fighting courageously in the most honorable battle of this era.

As for my younger brother, when I wanted to ask him to stay strong, he preceded me to calm me down, saying, 'Don’t be upset. Hassan is a hero.'

From Jenin camp, a message from  Mohammed Al-Amer on the first anniversary of the martyrdom of his eldest son, Ayser, who was followed by his brother, Ayham, a few days later:

"Ayser, the love of my heart... A year has passed, and you still dwell in my soul and conscience. A year in which I’ve tried to escape two images... pride... and pain. My beloved son, a year has passed, and my soul exudes the fragrance of your blood... and the memory of your manhood - of how you never compromised in the pursuit of highest. A year has passed, and before your flame extinguished, Ayham was the first to reach you. He joined you in your world, in that world where there was no room for humiliation - a world woven with threads of dignity. 

My beloved son, though I cannot deny that you, your brother, and all those who followed your path are crowns of pride upon our heads - you are the bearers of the embers of honor, and the kings of zero distance in preserving our honor and pride. You are those whom the world could not sway, nor were you halted by its trivial dreams. Your one dream was always to find a home in the sky, with the pure arms, rifles, and blood. You taught us that the path to honor is paved through the arms of the soil.

A year has passed, and I still remember that harsh night, illuminated by your blood... That night, I was your father and you were my child, whom I feared for. Ever since you were released from prison, I lived with you in the anticipation of farewell. In every confrontation, I awaited the day you would be carried on the shoulders of men. That night, I tried to escape myself, yet my heart and my eyes followed the news... that they were besieged at zero distance - that was the place Ayser chose. I wasn’t surprised by the news, but later I realized how precious you were to me. My heart shattered, though a part of my spirit remained to join you after a few days. Ayham, beloved of my soul, couldn’t bear the patience to wait. On that same path, he illuminated the journey to reach you with his blood. For the beloved does not reach the beloved except as a martyr.. Or a martyr... You left, filling my soul with love, pain, and pride. 

The entire world, the entire universe, does not equal my love and grief. But what consoles me, after God, is that you were the best of men, the most honorable of men, the first among men... and that you were the finest souls Allah gifted to me... and He, glory be to Him, out of His love for you, chose you.

I am satisfied with Allah's decree, and I am satisfied with you. I am filled with love and pride for you... You have made me the father of two moons, two lights that kindle the soul, heart, and faith. I entrust you and my heart and soul to the One whose safekeeping never fails.

- Abu Al-Ayser and Al-Ayham"

📌 Al-Akhbar Response to Mounting US Pressure

Since its founding in 2006, Al-Akhbar has gained attention for its investigative journalism and critiques of US influence in Lebanon, drawing repeated scrutiny from US officials. Initially, concerns centered on the newspaper's alleged funding. However, after its collaboration with WikiLeaks and the subsequent exposure of sensitive diplomatic documents, interest from the US increased significantly. Following Al-Aqsa Flood operation, the US embassy began probing into Al-Akhbar’s sources, accusing it of supporting groups like Hamas, Islamic Jihad, and Hezbollah. The US Treasury has also investigated possible American connections to the newspaper. 
 
Al-Akhbar has consistently reported on US involvement in Lebanon, particularly in the military, where American advisers exert significant influence. The newspaper has frequently criticized Commander Aoun’s leadership and his close ties to the US, prompting Aoun to seek clarification from Hezbollah on whether these critiques reflected the party's stance. Ultimately, he attributed the criticism to internal political disputes.
 
Recent revelations about US intelligence activities, including a potential role in the attempted assassination of Hezbollah official Wafiq Safa, have further escalated tensions. The US embassy is reportedly behind efforts to silence Al-Akhbar, considering sanctions, cyberattacks, and efforts to discredit the newspaper. Al-Akhbar has faced cyberattacks, suspected to be orchestrated by Israeli intelligence. In early October, the newspaper’s website suffered a major attack, suggesting state-level involvement. Meanwhile, Ambassador Lisa Johnson sought to rally Lebanese "friends" to condemn the newspaper, with pro-US voices predicting its downfall, claiming knowledge of plans to stifle it.
 
In response, Al-Akhbar’s editorial board holds the US embassy responsible for any harm to its staff or property, warning that any actions, including that taken by “Israel” against the newspaper would be seen as a direct assault by the US. Additionally, Army Commander General Joseph Aoun must ensure the embassy refrains from actions that could harm Al-Akhbar employees, as his team participated in discussions aimed at curbing the newspaper's criticism. Despite increasing pressure, the newspaper remains steadfast in resisting foreign interference and defending those opposing American and Israeli influence in Lebanon.

The eternal martyr Basil Al-Araj wrote abundantly before his ascension in March of 2017. In his posthumously published book, "I Have Found My Answers," (a line from his will) he spoke of war during the 2014 zionist aggression on Gaza, just prior to the IOF ground invasion on July 17.

Basil guided us with eight rules and insights on the nature of war. He wrote:
—
Since there is talk of a ground operation, several points must be considered:

1. The Palestinian resistance consists of guerrilla formations whose strategies follow the logic of guerrilla warfare or hybrid warfare, which Arabs and Muslims have become masters of through our experiences in Afghanistan, Iraq, Lebanon, and Gaza. War is never based on the logic of conventional wars and the defense of fixed points and borders; on the contrary, you draw the enemy into an ambush. You do not stick to a fixed position to defend it; instead, you perform maneuvers, movement, withdrawal, and attack from the flanks and the rear. So, never measure it against conventional wars.

2. The enemy will spread photos and videos of their invasion into Gaza, occupation of residential buildings, or presence in public areas and well-known landmarks. This is part of the psychological warfare in guerrilla wars; you allow your enemy to move as they wish so that they fall into your trap and you strike them. You determine the location and timing of the battle. So, you may see photos from Al-Katiba Square, Al-Saraya, Al-Rimal, or Omar Al-Mukhtar Street, but do not let this weaken your resolve. The battle is judged by its overall results, and this is merely a show.

3. Never spread the occupation's propaganda, and do not contribute to instilling a sense of defeat. This must be focused on, for soon, we will start talking about a massive invasion in Beit Lahia and Al-Nusseirat, for example. Never spread panic; be supportive of the resistance and do not spread any news broadcast by the occupation (forget about the ethics and impartiality of journalism; just as the zionist journalist is a fighter, so are you).

4. The enemy may broadcast images of prisoners, most likely civilians, but the goal is to suggest the rapid collapse of the resistance. Do not believe them.

5. The enemy will carry out tactical, qualitative operations to assassinate some symbols [of resistance], and all of this is part of psychological warfare. Those who have died and those who will die will never affect the resistance's system and cohesion because the structure and formations of the resistance are not centralized but horizontal and widespread. Their goal is to influence the resistance's support base and the families of the resistance fighters, as they are the only ones who can affect the men of the resistance.

6. Our direct human and material losses will be much greater than the enemy's, which is natural in guerrilla wars that rely on willpower, the human element, and the extent of patience and endurance. We are far more capable of bearing the costs, so there is no need to compare or be alarmed by the magnitude of the numbers.

7. Today's wars are no longer just wars and clashes between armies but rather are struggles between societies. Let us be like a solid structure and play a game of biting fingers with the enemy, our society against their society.

Finally, every Palestinian (in the broad sense, meaning anyone who sees Palestine as a part of their struggle, regardless of their secondary identities), every Palestinian is on the front lines of the battle for Palestine, so be careful not to fail in your duty.

The resistance here is not just a weapon in the hands of the men of God on the battlefields, but rather a spirit rooted in a society of sacrifices and loyalty.  It is an unbreakable momentum, and a steadfastness that knows no defeat.

🚨🟡🟢 Hezbollah Secretary-General Sayyed Hassan Nasrallah delivered a speech today, September 19th, 2024, following the zionist massacres of the last two days in Lebanon (2/3): 
—

Another goal of the enemy is to undermine the support base, wear it down, exhaust it, and make it cry out for the resistance to stop.

🚨🟡🟢 Hezbollah Secretary-General Sayyed Hassan Nasrallah delivered a speech today, September 19th, 2024, following the zionist massacres of the last two days in Lebanon (1/3): 
—
I congratulate Muslims on the occasion of the birth of the Greatest Messenger, Prophet Muhammad, and his grandson, Imam Jaafar Al-Sadiq.

I extend my highest congratulations to the families of the martyrs, both in the homeland and on the southern front, for receiving the honor of martyrdom, and I wish the wounded a speedy recovery.

I thank the government, the medical sector in Lebanon for their serious efforts, and all those who donated blood in various Lebanese regions.

I express gratitude to everyone who transported a wounded person and those who declared their readiness to donate organs to the injured.

I thank our kind people and our dear citizens from all regions, as well as political and labor leaders who stood in solidarity with us.

One of the blessings of what happened is the unprecedented humanitarian and moral epic we witnessed on both the national and humanitarian levels. 

Thanks to the countries that rushed to send medical aid and transport the injured, particularly Iraq and Iran, and to the Syrian government for opening its hospitals.

The enemy used a civilian device employed by large segments of society, and repeated this on Wednesday by detonating wireless communication devices, regardless of who was carrying them. As a result of this aggression, dozens of martyrs were killed, including children, women, and civilians.

Some explosions occurred in hospitals, markets, public roads, homes, and locations primarily populated by civilians.

When the enemy detonated the pagers, it deliberately aimed to kill 4,000 people in a single minute. 

Over the course of two days, on Tuesday and Wednesday, in just one minute, the enemy intended to kill no fewer than 5,000 people in two minutes.

The human efforts and the eagerness shown by the people contributed to reducing serious injuries. 

Through Allah's grace and with sincere and devoted human efforts, zeal, passion, and the massive presence of different segments of our people, a large part of the enemy's goal was thwarted.

We have formed technical and security investigation committees and are studying all possibilities. We have reached an almost complete conclusion, but the case is still under investigation and careful follow-up—from production to distribution and procedures in Lebanon. We will reach definitive results regarding the explosions in a short time, and appropriate measures will be taken accordingly.

There is no doubt that we were subjected to a major blow, both in terms of security and humanity, unprecedented in the history of the resistance in Lebanon, and also unprecedented in Lebanon, the conflict with the zionist enemy, and on a global scale. 

What happened was a major terrorist operation, and we will define the events of Tuesday and Wednesday as massacres.

This significant and powerful blow did not and will not bring us down.

This is the nature of war and conflict. We know that our enemy has technological superiority because the West stands behind it. When we engage in this conflict, we rely on effort, sacrifices, attrition, and the accumulation of gains to win. War is a cycle—one day for us from our enemy, and one day for our enemy from us.

Tuesday and Wednesday were a major test for us, and, Allah willing, we will overcome this test with pride. I say that this major blow will not bring us down. Through this experience and its lessons, we will become stronger, more steadfast, and more capable of confronting all possibilities and dangers.

The father of the martyr brothers Ayser and Ayham Al-Amer:

"Ayser was an affectionate young man and loving to those around him, as well as kind-hearted. Since the age of 16 years old, he had the spirit of leadership. He was influenced by his uncle Ziyad Al-Amer from a young age.

Ayham was sociable, fun, and loved by everyone.

Ayham and Ayser were attached since their childhood. Ayser got arrested, and after a while, Ayham also ended up in prison. Ayham naturally loved life, but after Ayser was martyred, Ayham didn't know how to go on living. Ayham changed; he used to care about his clothing and appearance.

Due to his sadness and attachment to Ayser, I didn't expect him to endure for long after Ayser's departure. Since their childhood, they were attached, studied together, slept in the same room, and even chose similar clothing. They were companions.

My relationship with Ayser and Ayham was more than a father-son relationship; it was a friendship that bound us together. Jokes, love, and closeness were present between us, which made the sorrow and pain for them even greater. Considering that I didn't just lose my sons, I lost my sons and my friends."

The Will of Martyr Mohammad Zakaria Al-Zubeidi:
—
"Indeed, Allah has purchased from the believers their lives and their properties [in exchange] for that they will have Paradise. They fight in the cause of Allah, so they kill and are killed. [It is] a true promise [binding] upon Him."

Praise be to Allah, Lord of the worlds, the supporter of the fighters and the humiliator of the Jews and the accursed ones. Peace and blessings be upon the leader of the pious and the commander of the fighters, and upon his family, companions, and those who follow his path and strive in His cause until the Day of Judgment. Furthermore..

In the Name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. Prayers and peace be upon the best of all servants, and the most honorable of messengers, our master Mohammed (PBUH). 

This is my final message to you. I am a young man, not yet 20, with many dreams and ambitions. I knew that, with Allah’s help, my dreams would come true. I loved life because I wanted to bring smiles to the faces of those I love. But what kind of life is this, where our women, children, and youth are killed unjustly, where Al-Aqsa and the Prophet’s sacred mosque are defiled while we remain asleep? Is it not shameful for us to sit idle?

To my dear father: I have fulfilled my wish and embraced martyrdom in the cause of Allah with the determination of the fighters. I have left this fleeting world, hastening to the eternal abode in the Gardens of Bliss, where I will meet the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, along with the prophets, the righteous, the martyrs, and the good. Know, my family, that I have not left you due to distress or loneliness, but martyrdom has called me, a call I have long awaited.

To my dear mother: Forgive me, for the satisfaction of the Lord is tied to your satisfaction. My wish will not be fulfilled without your patience and acceptance of my martyrdom as a martyred servant of Allah, a fighter in His cause, raising His word, and avenging the martyrs of Palestine. Do not cry over me, but ululate instead, for this is the wedding of your martyr son.

My will to you, my family, is that no one should cry at my wedding procession, but rather distribute dates and ululate in celebration of my martyrdom.

In conclusion, I say to you: Until we meet again soon in a paradise as vast as the heavens and the earth, prepared for the righteous.

 And our final prayer is that all praise is due to Allah, Lord of the worlds.

Your son and brother, the living martyr Mohammad Al-Zubeidi 'Abu Zakaria'

🚨 The Prisoners and Ex-Prisoners Affairs' Commission and the Palestinian Prisoner's Club mourn former prisoner Wafa Jarar (50 years old) from Jenin. She is the wife of administrative detainee Abdul Jabbar Jarrar, who has been held under administrative detention since February 2024. 

Wafa passed away this morning at Ibn Sina Hospital in Jenin, succumbing to critical injuries sustained during her arrest on May 21, 2024. This was her first arrest, and she was the mother of four children.

The Commission and the Club stated that the occupation executed a compounded crime against her, beginning with the brutal arrest from her home, during which she was severely beaten and injured, leading to the amputation of her legs above the knee at "Afula" Hospital. She suffered multiple injuries to her body and was later placed under administrative detention for four months. Jarrar was released on May 30, but continued to suffer. 

Thr occupation has not yet handed over the martyr Jarrar's amputated legs for burial according to Islamic law, despite filing requests to retrieve them after one of the doctors said they were "disposed of."

The occupation has also not responded to the request for her medical reports and to clarify what happened from May 27, 2024, the day she underwent below-knee amputation, to the subsequent above-knee amputation performed without her family's consent, as she was under anesthesia and on a ventilator.  

Wafa is one of dozens of detainees who have been subjected to systematic medical crimes since the start of the genocide war.
