Why do the “Arab Spring” liberals hate Sinwar?

Some parts of an article by writer Musa Al-Sada in Al-Akhbar:

History must record Yahya Sinwar as one of the smartest strategic revolutionaries in our Arab history, indeed in the history of resisting Western colonialism. Sinwar answered, amid the barrage of attacks he faced regarding the usefulness of the Great March of Return in Gaza (2018-2019), that there was a strategic purpose behind it, and that using peaceful means aimed to expose the contradictions of the world and its values (liberalism, from democracy to human rights, and more). Sinwar knew that the Palestinian person was excluded from these values, but he also knew that the Palestinian duty was to work on a historical and systematic multi-stage process against the falsity of liberalism, and that this battle was essential in the path of Palestinian and Arab liberation. Sinwar did not hide this, and within a media work program, he stated that with our blood we will expose the entire world and all the normalizers.

Sinwar placed the idol of Arab liberals, with all their approaches, before his eyes, and he addressed the people with resonant slogans, grand narratives, and a clear Palestinian goal. This type of speech annoys the "Arab Spring" liberals; to them, it is "populist" because it touches people's emotions, free from elitism. Most importantly, it speaks about political projects to change the reality of the entire people, while the liberal is an individualistic being who does not want to change the situation for the people and the nation but wants to change his own situation and lifestyle.

Furthermore, Sinwar, with his simplicity and modesty in dress and appearance, and his tongue that mirrors the people's speech, does not suit the class tastes of activists, cultural salons, democracy conferences, and academia. He was and still is a son of a refugee camp, in his harshness, anger, and improvisation when he speaks, qualities for which he has long been criticized.

But the most important aspect of all this is that Sinwar targeted, in what a friend called several years ago "burning the stages," and within his strategic preparation for the "flood," aimed to demolish the malicious narrative of the struggle of “democracy” against “tyranny” as a general reductionist cover for entrenchments, civil strife, identity conflicts, and self-destructive wars that deplete the nation's resources. He called for repositioning the Palestinians from the margins of events or involvement in Arab divisions to a pioneering role for Palestinians in correcting the Arab stance against zionism and behind it Western colonialism.

The historical process led by Sinwar aimed to expose the falsity of liberalism at its propagandistic level for the West: Zionism as an oasis of democracy, which the "Arab Spring" liberals have consistently defended, and then he worked on reinjecting the grand liberation slogans into Arab politics, like the slogans that Arab liberals inherited to weaken and mock generation after generation since Umm Kulthum sang "There is only one road to Palestine," calling for realism and solutions under the Western ceiling. This effort extended to addressing the Arab division where the "Spring" liberals vent their class and identity resentments, calling for a united word not to abandon Palestine, and for it to be the title of our collective liberation unity.

For all these reasons, they hate Sinwar, and for these reasons, we love him.

Bassem Khandaqji, a Palestinian prisoner who has been held for 20 years, has won the 2024 international Booker Prize for Arabic Fiction for his book "A Mask the Color of the Sky."

The novel was selected from among 133 novels. This recognition is a victory for the Palestinian voice and the prisoners, who continue to write and exchange information from the revolutionary schools inside zionist prisons amidst escalated torture and repression. 

Heroic prisoners such as Mahmoud Al-Aridah, Thaer Hammad, Hassan Salameh, and Abdullah Barghouti have all published books from within the prison walls: novels, reflections, historical accounts, and allegories for the struggle. Freed prisoner and leader Yahya Sinwar envisioned the future of liberation in his book "The Thorn and Carnation," while martyred leader Walid Daqqah wrote accounts of the Battle of Jenin or the psychological war on prisoners. A popular book among prisoners is Struggle From the Darkness, which sheds light on zionist psychological tactics during arrest, interrogation, and imprisonment and how to stand steadfast against them. 

The literary productions of the prisoners have been among the most prominent tools of struggle armed by the prisoners, despite all efforts by the occupation to erase the Palestinian narrative and voice.

Khandaqji (40 years old), from Nablus, was arrested in November 2004. In his last 20 years in prison, he has published two poetry collections, 250 articles, and several novels, a number of which have been translated to French. 

He was subjected to a harsh interrogation, followed by a sentence of three life terms when he was 20 years old while he was a journalism and media student at Al-Najah University.

When his novel was nominated for the Booker Prize, Bassem was subjected to a failed smear campaign by zionist media. Yet, Bassem left his mark, contributing to the mountain of Palestinian literature despite his imprisonment and overcoming the challenges of publication from prison.

🚨 For the 32nd month, 51-year-old prisoner Yaqoub Qadri has been detained in solitary confinement in the zionist prisons, according to the Prisoners Affairs Commission. 

Yaqoub has been detained since 2003 and sentenced to life imprisonment. Since his re-arrest after he broke out of Gilboa prison in September 2021, he has been in solitary confinement for 32 consecutive months. He and five other prisoners succeeded to breach the security system at Gilboa prison on September 7th 2021, executing a heroic escape in the Freedom Tunnel operation and roamed across Palestine for two weeks before being re-arrested. 

Revenge against Qadri began from the moment he was re-arrested, but escalated against all prisoners since October 7th 2023, as he was transferred several times between the cells of "Ohli Kedar, Ashkelon, and Rimonim" prisons. Throughout all this, the jailers insulted him, threatened him with death and murder, and stated that he would be taken out of the prison in a black bag.

Prisoner Yaqoub suffers from several diseases including thyroid issues, high blood pressure, cholesterol, eye and dental problems, constant headaches, hand and stomach pains, and continuous fatigue. The Commission's lawyer was only allowed to visit him for 15 minutes after a long argument with the prison administration, which then granted some extra time.

Qadri told the Commission's lawyer, "The conditions are difficult and complex as in other prisons, cell searches are conducted day and night, hot water is only available for ten minutes, electricity runs from 6 PM until the early morning hours, we are deprived of clothing and blankets, the food is poor in quantity and quality, drinking water is filled from a bathroom faucet, and we are only allowed out for an hour a day for exercise, and all the jailers wear masks and do not reveal their faces. - He was among 23 prisoners whom the occupation refused to release in all exchange deals. The occupation also withdrew from placing him on the list of released long-term prisoners in 2013-2014.
- He is considered one of the most prominent prisoner theorists and thinkers inside the occupation prisons. He has intellectual and literary productions that have reached the international level, the most famous of which are the books “The Melting of Consciousness” and “Parallel Time,” and the novel “The Story of the Secret of Oil,” which won international fame and many awards,  continued with the novel “The Story of the Secret Sword,” a second part, and it was expected that the third part, “The Tale of the Secret of the Spectrum,” would be published.
- His book, Fusion of Consciousness or “Redefining Torture,” is considered one of the most prominent productions of the prisoner movement, and is considered an important reference. Through this study, he laid out the ABCs of steadfastness, discipline, rooting organizational work, and the strength of the will inside prisons. He is also credited with crystallizing concepts that describe the reality inside prisons, such as the concept of “Parallel Time” (i.e., the time of prisoners versus the time of those outside the prisons), and he wrote a large number of political, intellectual, and literary articles and studies, and is considered a first-class political thinker. He produced inside the occupation prisons a huge intellectual project during which he answered all issues, inquiries, and existential questions, as well as issues of liberation, strengthening the state of criticism within it, on his way to reaching the truth and the crisis of the national project. He was a painter who provided the prison with many important national paintings, speaking the Hebrew language fluently.
- He was subjected to torture, solitary confinement, and being prevented from visiting during his long detention, and his writings and publications were pursued by the Prison Service.
- He smuggled sperm out of prison, became a father at the age of 57, and gave birth to a daughter he called “Milad.”
- On December 18, 2022, it was announced that he was suffering from a rare bone marrow cancer, as a result of which his health condition deteriorated and he was transferred to the hospital. In June 2023, his request for release was rejected, and the Central Court also rejected the appeal he submitted against the committee’s decision.
- He died this evening as a result of a policy of medical negligence, after a serious deterioration in his health.

As the Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine bids farewell to its comrade, the leader, thinker, writer, inspiration and great theoretician, it pledges to him to be loyal to his national, intellectual and Front legacy, through which he made Palestine and the cause of its liberation his compass. Until his departure, he remained inhabited by Palestine, all of Palestine from its river to its sea.

Glory to the great martyr of Palestine and humanity.

We will certainly be victorious.

The Popular Front extends to the comrade leader, Secretary-General, Ahmed Saadat, the comrades in the prison branch and the prisoner movement, his struggling wife, Sanaa, his daughter Milad, his entire family, and all his comrades, its deepest condolences on the martyrdom of this comrade, leader and inspiration, one of the generals of steadfastness, and the distinguished national and Front leaders and writers who engraved their name in letters of gold for what he presented in rich and inspiring struggle experience for many generations of prisoners in the occupation prisons. Biography of Comrade Commander Walid Daqqah “Abu Milad”:
- Born on July 18, 1961.
- He is from the town of Baqa al-Gharbiya, Haifa district.
- Commander Walid grew up in a Palestinian family consisting of 6 brothers and 3 sisters.
- The martyr received his basic education in Baqa al-Gharbiya schools, and obtained his high school diploma in 1979 from Yimma Agricultural Secondary School. He joined the university and continued his scientific and academic studies.
- During his studies, his views and awareness of the issues of his people and his deep affiliation to his Palestinian national identity blossomed.
- In 2010, he obtained a bachelor’s degree in the interdisciplinary study of democracy, and in 2016 he obtained a master’s degree in regional studies, “Israeli Studies track” from Al-Quds University, but he was unable to complete his preparation for the degree of PhD.
- He joined the ranks of the Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine in 1983, and joined a military cell affiliated with the Front.
- In 1984, he received military training at the Front’s military bases in Syria, and then he contributed to the formation of a secret military apparatus for the Front inside the occupied interior, whose mission was to collect information about zionist leaders and officials who participated in committing massacres in the invasion of Lebanon.
- The martyr Walid and his comrades within the military cell carried out a series of operations, including the kidnapping and killing of the zionist soldier “Moshe Tammam”, as a result of which he and a group of comrades were arrested and sentenced to life imprisonment.

🟡 Allah SWT loves to listen to our Duaa; He wants to hear us pray and supplicate and ask Him for our every need. He wants us to repeat our calls and requests and go into the details of our requests time and time again and to never despair.

🟡 The Quran tells us stories of the prophets (PBUT) and how they supplicated to Allah all the time. Prophet Ibrahim (PBUH) was referred to as "one who supplicates [to Allah] a lot."

🟡 In a Hadeeth Qudsi, Allah SWT tells Prophet Musa (AS) to ask Him whatever he needs, even the salt he would need for his dough.

The Prince of the Shadows - Imminent Freedom (3/3):
One day, while searching for an apartment with a fake ID, he was noticed by zionist agent who owned a real estate office. The agent didn't recognize him by name, but by his facial features and a camera in the office. At the next apartment showing, a trap was set up to abduct Abdullah. 

On this morning 21 years ago, March 5th 2003, he planned to take his daughter Tala to the doctor for treatment before his apartment appointment. Since all his friends were wanted by the occupation and his wife was sick, he had to go by himself to treat her. The doctor was late, so he was forced to take Tala with him to the appointment. 

As soon as he arrived at the parking lot with his daughter Tala in his arms, two police dogs attacked him, so he threw his daughter into the car and locked it, trying to fend them off. One of them started biting his leg and the other his winter jacket. He was able to get rid of the dogs, but when he looked up, a group of occupation soldiers surrounded him, pointing their machine guns at him, threw him to the ground, handcuffed him, and took him to a car nearby.

His interrogation alone lasted 5 entire months with intense torture. In November, his sentence was announced: 67 life sentences plus 5,200 years, the longest sentence in history.

He wrote of his torture:
"Handcuffed and shackled, suspended...from the ceiling of the cell at the elbow,
Dawn of bullets without a rising sun... without hope, and eyes that weep.
Occupation imposes, and inverts logic... a question and interrogation, then an inquiry.
My body aches and their whips are like fire... bones break and bones are crushed.
My sea is stormy and my thoughts drown... my heart aches, and I feel suffocated.
I was captured and tortured, yet the pawn did not fall... my soul ascended in agony to the Creator.
No, my soul did not ascend to the heavens... I did not become a martyr, and still, I stare at the interrogator."

In 2015, he was interviewed with a smuggled phone. He urged Hamas not to rush on a prisoner exchange deal then. "We are patient," he said, "and will continue to be so even if we'd be released in a thousand years. The prisoners are ready to be patient. Steadfast. Steadfast." He was punished with solitary confinement, which he protested with a hunger strike. 

From prison, he resists with the pen, having written 12 books, including a novel, the Prince of Shadows, about his life. It begins with a letter to his daughter Tala, who witnessed the moment of his arrest. He asks, "Who are you? and why are you?"

In just three years, Abdullah changed the face of the resistance. He strengthened it with his outside knowledge and enhanced it with his teachings, which continue to bear fruit today. Abdullah created not only bombs, but minds—training, developing, and evolving. 

Perhaps it will be 1,000 years until Abdullah sees freedom. Or, perhaps, the legacy of Abdullah Ghaleb Al-Barghouti, marked by his profound impact on the resistance, will ignite the flame of liberation, bringing his freedom sooner than the world anticipates. All the while, Abdullah remains in his cell, waiting, steadfast, and principled.

The Prince of the Shadows - Return to Palestine (2/3):
Soon after, Abdullah returned to Beit Rima in Ramallah, Palestine on a visitation permit due to his lack of Palestinian ID. 

Abdullah was shocked by what he saw. His homeland was littered with zionist flags, with collaborators and spies. He muttered in Korean: "I swear that I will be the reason this whole land is liberated." Fortunately, he got married, and had three children, Safaa, Tala and Osama. But, Abdullah nearly forgot the promise that he made to himself while working on his own life and business.

Then, the second intifada exploded. He knew that his experience from outside would prove to be a true hidden talent.

On one of these days, a road was blocked because two bus drivers were fighting. They blasphemed in Abdullah's eyes, so Abdullah grabbed one and beat him until he broke his face, then beat the second bus driver. He told the passengers, "Whoever insults God in front of me is on an appointment with death. Whoever doesn't like it, my name is Abdullah Al-Barghouti, and I live in Deir Ghassan." An old, astonished man approached him, thanking him and inviting him for dinner. Abdullah wanted to leave before the old man stopped him: "My son, don't forget about the trust I promised you." He guided him to a spot in the ground and told him to dig into it. There, he found a suitcase, which he vowed to protect.

It wasn't any suitcase. It was the suitcase of the Engineer that came before him, the suitcase of martyr Yahya Ayyash, whose operations and explosives shook the core of the entity. Yahya left the suitcase with the man as he was pursued by the IOF before his martyrdom. "Finish the path of Yahya Ayyash, my son."

Abdullah quietly hacked zionist websites, networks, and surveillance systems after this moment. He planned and waited, always patient.

One day, he heard on the news of an IOF assassination of a resistance leader with a remotely-detonated telephone explosive. Abdullah vowed to create the same device. He took out Yahya's suitcase, which he had never opened. Inside, he found two explosives. He dismantled them, reverse engineered them, and built the same explosive anew after three failed trials. To test it, he placed it on the ears of the neighborhood donkey named "Sharon's Donkey." He called the donkey, heard his bray on the line, and set off the powerful explosive. 

It worked very well. Abdullah was proud of what he achieved without any help and continued to manufacture explosives alone. 

Abdullah watched the security cameras he hacked, noticing his cousin Bilal masked with a kuffiyeh, writing resistance slogans on walls with a group of youth. He was certain that his cousin was the way into the resistance. 

In Palestine, no one knew of his engineering expertise. On a day in May, Abdullah told his cousin Bilal that he wanted to show him something. He took him to a quiet area near Beit Rima and detonated one of his very small explosives. Bilal was shocked by the explosion it created. "You can do that and didn't tell us?!," Bilal shouted. Bilal ran to Nablus to tell commander Ayman Halawa of the Al-Qassam Brigades of his cousin's abilities, returning to Abdullah to ask him to join the Al-Qassam Brigades immediately.

From then on, Abdullah produced everything from devices to detonators in his private laboratory in his town. From here, the Engineer orchestrated operations that killed 67 zionist settlers and wounded over 500, including the "Sbarro" operation. Abdullah even trained resistance fighters in taekwondo and judo in courses.

The Prince of the Shadows - A Diasporic Upbringing (1/3):
Abdullah Ghaleb Al-Barghouti is one of the most formidable resistance leaders of our time. Born and raised in Kuwait in 1972, he spent just a total of 3 years of his life with the resistance, but those 3 years changed the face of the resistance as we know it.

Before his return to Palestine, he became fluent in Korean and English, worked in Korea, and became an engineering expert, earning black belts in judo and taekwondo on his path to Palestine.

In Kuwait, Abdullah studied hard, but he was bullied by his peers and teachers. He took up judo to defend himself, soon earning his black belt. His teacher even taught him moves that could kill, telling him not to use them in practice. He told him, "I taught you these moves to use against the zionists, because you are Palestinian." 

At this stage, Abdullah barely considered himself Palestinian, having been raised in Kuwait and never seeing his land. With his judo teacher's comment, and seeing his family's pride at the martyrdom of his two cousins in Palestine, a switch flipped inside Abdullah, who found renewed loyalty to his homeland and cause.

With the outbreak of the First Gulf War, Abdullah was jailed for a month for resisting American forces. He soon settled in Jordan with his family, where Abdullah opened a mechanic shop.

After accumulating a $5000 debt after opening the shop, he travelled to South Korea a year later to continue his education in order to find a job to pay it off. He spent days without food, water, or rest. He pleaded to work in a factory, working a job that paid 8 times more what he would have made in Jordan. In a few months, he paid off his debt.

In the same room of the factory where he slept, a lone computer sat in the corner. He watched it daily until he was able to obtain the password. With internet access, he taught himself Korean and hacked networks in order to make international phone calls for free. With his limited access, he learned to create bombs and rockets, which he would experiment with in the Korean forest.

In Korea, Abdullah was not able to finish his degree, but he learned much electrical engineering, including the design and manufacture of satellite receivers. He planned to sell vehicles in Jordan upon his return, all while continuing to practice martial arts. At this time, he picked up taekwondo on top of judo. 

In 1988, protests broke out in South Korea after US Forces assaulted a Korean girl. Abdullah joined the protests, and was arrested while throwing Molotov cocktails. After 5 years in Korea, he was forced to return to Amman with his Korean wife, where he worked as an electronic engineer. 

Not long passed until Abdullah and his wife divorced due to her choice not to bear children. Abdullah became a new man after this moment. One relative living in Spain visited him at the time, witnessing a number of items from his life in Korea in his room; she threw them out without his knowledge, replacing them with Palestinian cultural items, turning it into a Palestinian home.

[1/2] 

In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.
Peace and blessings be upon you, my master, my lord, the ultimate goal of my desires, and the beloved of the Lord of the worlds, Muhammad bin Abdullah, and upon his purified and chosen progeny.

This message is the testament of the lowly one who spent all his days in heedlessness and distance from Allah, the Almighty. But by His mercy, love, and grace upon the negligent sinners, and by instilling in my heart a longing to see the glory of His noble face, and inspiring me with infatuation in the reflection of His face, Mohammed, peace be upon him and his family, and granting me the burning anguish and sorrow for the tragedy of my master Abu Abdullah Al-Hussein (as). All of this is from His kindness, grace, and favor upon me, as He has chosen me to be among those who adhere to the path of sacred love among the predecessors who fulfilled what they promised. So, I ask Allah, the Almighty, to place me in the position of servants of my master, the Messenger of Allah, and his purified progeny.

My will to you, my brothers, is to forgive your negligent brother and servant who has caused harm and negligence towards you. I beseech you, by the right of my mistress Fatimah, to pardon me and forgive me, knowing that I do not deserve it.

I implore you to graciously remember me in your prayers and visits. My special request is that you remember me in the gatherings of Al-Hussein (as), even with a tear that may alleviate the horrors that the sinful servant will suffer due to his deeds in his life.

Do not abandon the gatherings of Imam Hussein (as), and do not shame anyone in these gatherings. Regardless of the form of the gathering, it is the servants of Imam Hussein who honor us with the mention of our Master Abu Abdullah. 

I hope from your esteemed selves not to abandon the Husseiniyah of Sayyida Amena bint Wahab, perhaps the remembrance and condolences held there will be an expiation for my sins and shortcomings. Do not abandon the path of Wilayat Al-Faqih and the path of Hezbolah. It is through this path, with its blessings, that we continue to live with dignity and honor, until we reach the handing over of the banner to our Master, the Remainder of Allah on His earth, Al-Mahdi, son of Al-Hassan, may Allah hasten his noble reappearance.

And do not betray the blood of the martyrs that was shed so that we may live in pride and steadfastness. 

I request from the caretakers of my grave not to place any gravestone, decorations, or marble until Allah permits the construction of a shrine for Sayyida Fatima (as).

And I hope that anything will be planted above my grave so it may alleviate its horrors upon me. Recite elegies and ziyarah, and I hope that a gathering of lamentation will be held at my grave, perhaps Fatimah will graciously honour me with a visit. 

To my family: forgive me, for I have been falling short and distant from you, but Allah is my witness to how much I love you. And I hope you do not burden your lives with enmity and animosity towards anyone, even if they wrong you; for nothing in this world is valuable or significant. And I hope you take care of my children. And do not object to any decision made concerning me. 

To all those who wronged and hurt me: Allah is my witness that I love you, and I have forgiven you from my heart, and I used to pray for you in the night prayer.

To my daughters Fatima Al-Hawraa and Fatima Al-Zahra: I love you both and I thank you both for you were the reason for the presence of the name Fatima (as), in the house. Carry the name of Sayyida Fatima (as) and preserve it, and know that I am always with you and I have not left you for a moment, and I will not leave you. Stay together, supporting each other. May Allah never separate you; I love you.

To Narjis and Sarah: Be well, and remember what I have entrusted you with.

Jamal Abu Samhadana and his companions remained: Abdul Karim Al-Quqa and Ismail Abu Al-Qumsan, who are former employees of the PA’s security services, in addition to Amer Qarmout (Abu Al-Saed - one of the former cadres of the Popular Front), Zuhair Al-Qaisi and Imad Hammad. Abdul Karim al-Quqa was appointed military commander-in-chief of al-Nasser Salah al-Din Brigades, while Commander Abu Ataya took charge of the leader of the Popular Resistance Committees. Al-Nasser Salah al-Din Brigades received great military support from Hezbollah.

The Formation of PRC and Its Military Wing

When the second intifada entered a new phase of confrontation and the military cells of the Palestinian factions beginning to form, a number of Abu Ataya's comrades, who had organizational affiliation, set out to establish a military operation of their own. Abu Shammala and al-Attar went to Izz al-Din al-Qassam Brigades (Hamas). Then Abdel-Al, Sheikh Khalil, Khaled Awaja and Khaled Mansour headed to the Al-Quds Brigades (PIJ). Meanwhile, Jamal Abdel-Razek went to form the armed military action within the framework of the Fatah movement, and what was later known as the Al-Aqsa Martyrs Brigades.

Since the beginning of those days, the martyrs Jamal Abu Samhadana, Khaled Awaja, and Mohammad Sheikh Khalil, along with a number of armed cadres, such as Awad al-Nayrab and Abu Ibrahim al-Qaisi, stormed the Rafah municipality building on September 28, 2000, and managed to seize a number of bulldozers, and opened fire. Martyr Samhadana and his companions, Abdul Karim al-Quqa (Abu Yusef), Imad Hammad, Abu Jamal al-Shaer, and Abu Ibrahim al-Qaisi, also worked on manufacturing hand grenades and simple explosive devices, and began their operations of targeting zionist enclaves. Meanwhile, the popular and organizational element was not absent from the life of Jamal Abu Samhadana, so he used the house of his father, as an office to attract new members and recruit them for organized resistance, before the formation of the Popular Resistance Committees.

The History Before PRC

On February 2000, the history of struggle and the desire for resistance brought these names together: Jamal Abu Samhadana, Raed Al-Attar, Mohammad Abu Shammala, Mohammad Sheikh Khalil, Abdel-Al, Khaled Mansour, Khaled Awaja, and Jamal Abdel-Razek. They organized themselves into a small and focused group that owned a few Kalashnikov. The group was concerned with the qualitative operations, such as shooting at settler cars and targeting checkpoints with a very simple and completely secret military organization. Their military development was influenced by the victory of the resistance in Lebanon, on May 2000. On September, in the same year, the second intifada broke out.

Abu Ataya supported various resistance groups continuously - particularly al-Quds Brigades and al-Qassam Brigades. The house of Jamal Abu Samhadana was a safe haven for a number of resistance fighters, such as Mohammad Al-Sanwar, Ibrahim Al-Maqadmeh, and Yahya Ayyash, who stayed for two weeks in his house.

The Unwavering and His Strong Compass

Jamal Abu Samhadana reformed his way to stay loyal to the path of anti-normalization and armed resistance until liberation. His meeting in 1993 with PIJ founder Dr. Fathi Shiqaqi, in Algeria, was said to be the seed of his resistance mission. Since when he worked in PA, he secretly supported the military wing of PIJ with his companion, former military leader of Fatah who became the military leader in PIJ, martyr Mohammad Sheikh Khalil. In 1995, Jamal Abu Samhadana was arrested and dismissed from his position in PA - which was his first persecution by it - after they found that him and martyr leader Mohammad Atwa Abdel-Al were involved in Beit Lid martyrdom operation. In 1996, martyr Jamal filled the secretariat of  Fatah in Rafah. The position didn't prevent the leader Jamal from continuing his struggle, and he tried to form a new reform movement within it, but the efforts failed. The noble martyr began to hold the need to find an effective alternative struggle.

17 years ago today, on June 8, 2006, Abu Ataya - the founder of Popular Resistance Committees and its military wing al-Nasser Salah al-Din Brigades - was assassinated by zionists. His leadership, his devotion for the liberation of Palestine, and his luminous character remain as the compass of the struggle for his own people. Other than his incredible military experience of a lifetime, he was known for his sincerity and warmness. The zionist occupation will never be able to "assassinate" the mark that Jamal Abu Samhadana left. ❤️‍🩹

Liberated prisoner Ruba Assi:
—
We know that all of these sacrifices will not go in vain. These sacrifices will not stop because of a truce or aid. 

When the resistance demands something, it encompasses everything. They think of all of the Palestinian people. They don't just think about the Gaza Strip and how to improve the living conditions there. They think about how to liberate Palestine from the river to the sea. 

The conditions of imprisonment are very difficult. The achievements of the prisoners, baptized in blood after long years of struggle, were taken away from them. 

From the first moments of the war, we had no food for long periods of time. We would sleep on the ground without mattresses. We were subjected to naked searches, beatings by the jailers for all female prisoners, and deprivation of food and water and all the necessities of life. We would sleep hungry. 

We do not strive for better prison conditions. We strive for freedom.

Liberated prisoner and Birzeit University student Ruba Assi has been abducted by the IOF several times for her student activism, having recently served 21 months until she was freed in March of last year until she was re-arrested after October 7th and placed under administrative detention without charge or trial. 

Today, she is freed from the zionist prisons again as a result of the resistance's prisoner exchange deal.

Today, Ruba declares, "We renew our faith in the Palestinian resistance in all places of their presence. This present freedom will extend to the complete freedom for Palestine and its people." She describes the 24/7 repression in the prisons and the constant steadfastness of the prisoners. 

"There is hope in the inevitability of victory, hope of freedom, not just for the prisoners, but for all Palestinian people and for all Palestinian lands."

"All the sacrifices that passed by the bodies of the martyrs will not go to waste. The occupation will go to waste, and it deserves it."

"We have complete faith in the inevitability of victory: today, tomorrow, or after it. We are all walking on this path, because we have faith in the resistance. We either live in dignity or die. It is better to die than live humiliated."

"All the Palestinian people are just waiting for the signal from the resistance."

Ruba reiterates what many liberated prisoners have been saying: prisoners are prevented from receiving news, or seeing their family or lawyers; the prison administration would lie to them before they were released. The prisoners would sleep without mattresses and were beaten daily. Yet, Ruba reiterates that the prisoners' confrontation with the jailer is constant.

"It is either us or them. Our life with them is impossible. We were not asking for just better prison conditions. We want freedom!"

The Prisoner Club stated that Said Nakhla has been arrested a total of 14 times since 1977, and spent a cumulative period of about 18 years, mostly under administrative detention. Nakhla suffers from multiple health problems resulting from the repeated arrests.

The Commission called for his urgent transfer to a civilian hospital that can provide him with the necessary medical treatments, warning against the risks of keeping him in the Ramla Clinic due to its lack of adequate medical facilities.

🇧🇭 Hundreds of political detainees in Jau Prison of Bahrain begin a hunger strike under the slogan 'We Have the Right'. 

In a statement:

"In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.

O Allah, bless Muhammad and the family of Muhammad, and hasten their relief and curse their enemies, both the earlier and the later, until the Day of Judgment.

We have a right that must be granted, or else we will endure the impossible. Even if the journey is long, we will persist.

The captive movement in our Jau Central Prison in Bahrain is witnessing a phase of its rightful struggle. The prison administration, under direct orders from the Bahraini authorities, has violated the most basic rights, even those granted to prisoners of war, disregarding international treaties and its own constitution that outlines the rights of prisoners registered with the prison administration.

All of this has been violated and trampled upon, disregarding the rights of prisoners and even their human dignity, which has been crushed repeatedly by unjust decisions, including:

1: Unjust solitary confinement for several prisoners, depriving them of many of their rights, including the freedom to practice religious rituals. Prolonged periods of isolation for trivial reasons, during which daily psychological and physical humiliation is practiced.

2: The suffocating daily program that keeps prisoners locked in their cells twenty-three hours a day, with only one hour outside for communication, clothing, exercise, and exposure to sunlight. Prisoners are not allowed to attend congregational prayers in the prison mosque.

3: The oppressive visitation system with a glass barrier and a reduction in the time and number of visits. Even uncles, aunts, and even the siblings of the prisoners are prohibited from visiting.

We also do not forget our lost right to education due to deliberate political ignorance, as well as our right to healthcare. The deteriorating health situation is evident to everyone, and the testimony of the martyr Mohammed Al-Aali is the best evidence.

Therefore, after exhausting all avenues to reclaim our rights, we have decided to take the next step:

An open hunger strike under the slogan 'We Have the Right,' inspired by the words of Amirul Mo'mineen (Imam Ali as) and drawing from the blessings of Ashura of Imam Hussein (as).

Our demands are not recreational but extremely necessary and fundamental to human life, even at the lowest levels witnessed in human history.

Our courageous and steadfast people are called upon to stand united with this movement both in the media and on the ground.

As the noble Ayatollah Qassim said: Their prison is a prison for all of us, and their ordeal is our ordeal. Anything that touches our dignity touches the dignity of our entire people. Therefore, let us preserve our dignity.

May Allah grant us success.

⬛ Issued by the detainees of Bahrain in Jau Central Prison - Coordinating Committee."

🟡 We take pride in being called the "Axis of Rejection" for this means that we are not slaves or tools for the Americans. We are dignified and honorable. Some people use this label as a derogatory term, but we do not see it as so.

🟡 The Americans will only lead us to a disastrous and painful reality if this deep interference in Lebanese matters continues. There are many sources that could generate money for the Lebanese Treasury, yet they are banned as a result of the "veto" of the American embassy.

Al-Nasser Salah al-Din Brigades received great military support from Hezbollah.

The Formation of PRC and Its Military Wing

When the second intifada entered a new phase of confrontation and the military cells of the Palestinian factions beginning to form, a number of Abu Ataya's comrades, who had organizational affiliation, set out to establish a military operation of their own. Abu Shammala and al-Attar went to Izz al-Din al-Qassam Brigades (Hamas). Then Abdel-Al, Sheikh Khalil, Khaled Awaja and Khaled Mansour headed to the Al-Quds Brigades (PIJ). Meanwhile, Jamal Abdel-Razek went to form the armed military action within the framework of the Fatah movement, and what was later known as the Al-Aqsa Martyrs Brigades.

Since the beginning of those days, the martyrs Jamal Abu Samhadana, Khaled Awaja, and Mohammad Sheikh Khalil, along with a number of armed cadres, such as Awad al-Nayrab and Abu Ibrahim al-Qaisi, stormed the Rafah municipality building on September 28, 2000, and managed to seize a number of bulldozers, and opened fire. Martyr Samhadana and his companions, Abdul Karim al-Quqa (Abu Yusef), Imad Hammad, Abu Jamal al-Shaer, and Abu Ibrahim al-Qaisi, also worked on manufacturing hand grenades and simple explosive devices, and began their operations of targeting zionist enclaves. Meanwhile, the popular and organizational element was not absent from the life of Jamal Abu Samhadana, so he used the house of his father, as an office to attract new members and recruit them for organized resistance, before the formation of the Popular Resistance Committees.

The History Before PRC

On February 2000, the history of struggle and the desire for resistance brought these names together: Jamal Abu Samhadana, Raed Al-Attar, Mohammad Abu Shammala, Mohammad Sheikh Khalil, Abdel-Al, Khaled Mansour, Khaled Awaja, and Jamal Abdel-Razek. They organized themselves into a small and focused group that owned a few Kalashnikov. The group was concerned with the qualitative operations, such as shooting at settler cars and targeting checkpoints with a very simple and completely secret military organization. Their military development was influenced by the victory of the resistance in Lebanon, on May 2000. On September, in the same year, the second intifada broke out.

Abu Ataya supported various resistance groups continuously - particularly al-Quds Brigades and al-Qassam Brigades. The house of Jamal Abu Samhadana was a safe haven for a number of resistance fighters, such as Mohammad Al-Sanwar, Ibrahim Al-Maqadmeh, and Yahya Ayyash, who stayed for two weeks in his house.

The Unwavering and His Strong Compass

Jamal Abu Samhadana reformed his way to stay loyal to the path of anti-normalization and armed resistance until liberation. His meeting in 1993 with PIJ founder Dr. Fathi Shiqaqi, in Algeria, was said to be the seed of his resistance mission. Since when he worked in PA, he secretly supported the military wing of PIJ with his companion, former military leader of Fatah who became the military leader in PIJ, martyr Mohammad Sheikh Khalil. In 1995, Jamal Abu Samhadana was arrested and dismissed from his position in PA - which was his first persecution by it - after they found that him and martyr leader Mohammad Atwa Abdel-Al were involved in Beit Lid martyrdom operation. In 1996, martyr Jamal filled the secretariat of  Fatah in Rafah. The position didn't prevent the leader Jamal from continuing his struggle, and he tried to form a new reform movement within it, but the efforts failed. The noble martyr began to hold the need to find an effective alternative struggle.

17 years ago today, on June 8, 2006, Abu Ataya - the founder of Popular Resistance Committees and its military wing al-Nasser Salah al-Din Brigades - was assassinated by zionists. His leadership, his devotion for the liberation of Palestine, and his luminous character remain as the compass of the struggle for his own people. Other than his incredible military experience of a lifetime, he was known for his sincerity and warmness. Although Bahjat al-‘Arifin is not among us, the brilliance of his splendor can still be felt. Rather, when does the splendor of such a lamp ever fade? Which lamp was lit by the teachings of Muhammad and His infallible progeny (peace be upon them), and did not turn immortal and eternal?

- Muhammad Khalfan

'Bahjat al-Aarifin'
The Splendor of the Gnostics

Written by Sheikh Muhammad M. Khalfan

He was known as Bahjat al-‘Aarifin (the splendor of the Gnostics) and profusely emanated the light of devotion, such that lovers throughout the world were fascinated by his noble spirit. He was well-known for his absorption in prayer, where the true munajat (mutual whispering) between the lover and the Beloved is clearly revealed. 

In most cases, the awe and veneration of the Infinite would apparently overcome him and he would weep from the depths of his heart in prayer. Although the world came to know him after a long period of his life of splendor, this elevated spirit was known to enjoy great stations of spiritual perfection since his youth. He personified religion in both its exoteric as well as esoteric dimensions. Exoterically he revealed his brilliance as a faqih (referral authority in Divine laws), and esoterically he exhibited the splendor of an absorbed mystic in the alley of the beloved. 

One of the significant things which powerfully emanated from that great ‘aarif was his steadfastness on the path of the only Beloved. The Holy Qur’an says: “Indeed those who say our Lord is Allah, and then are steadfast, angels descend on them saying: Fear not, nor be grieved, and receive good news of the garden which you were promised…” (41:30)

Some of his spiritual teachings to those thirsty in pursuing the path of spiritual development are as follows:

1- There is no litany [ dhikr - word of remembering God] more exalted then the litany of action; and there is no litany of action higher than abandoning sin in [one’s] beliefs and acts of devotion. And apparently, abandoning sin in the absolute sense without continual self-vigilance cannot take place.

2- Among the dictums of Imam Ali (‘A) is: “Know that every action of yours is according to your prayer (sala).” You must protect your five times daily prayer in the beginning of their times and with total inclination to Him (Almighty is He), and God-willing you will not lose felicity.

3- We must deal with [the holy] traditions and read them, for [the] cure is in them.

4- If a person realizes the purpose of creation, he would find it extremely sweet to [die as a martyr and] be restored back to life seventy times.

5- The prayer of night (salat al-layl) is the key to occasions of Divine succor (tawfiqaat)

6- It is these very supererogatory (mustahabb) acts that make the human being attain a great station.

7- The reason why we are left behind is our consumption of doubtful wealth; and doubtful wealth brings about doubts and ambiguity.

8- God knows when a human being send greetings to Muhammad and his progeny (i.e. recites salawat) one time and gifts that to the deceased soul, what kind of meaning, form and reality it has!

When a scholar of the caliber of Ayatollah Muhammad Taqi Bahjat (may Allah elevate his noble spirit) tears the curtains of this material world and releases the bird of his spirit from the cage of the corporeal body, the moths of love residing in this terrestrial realm suffer a loss which cannot be recuperated. Bahjat al-‘Arifeen not only served as a lamp for expounding the Shari’a (the Islamic laws), but also as a master for guiding towards the tariqa (the path) which leads to the haqiqa (the Ultimate Reality). 

The human being fundamentally has been brought in this transient world for this very purpose. The word shari’a which we commonly translate as the Islamic law literally signifies mawrid al-maa’ al-jaari (‘the entrance of flowing water’). When we say shari’at al-Furat, for example, we mean “the entrance of the flowing waters of Euphrates”. But Religion only serves as the shari‘a for those of us who would really like to “enter the flowing spiritual waters” and benefit from the same so that we may attain the haqiqa (Absolute Reality).

Martyr Mustafa Badreddine in the eyes of his daughter, Zahra, from Mehr News:

Q: How was your relationship with the martyr and how was he performing his duties as a parent?

My father was known for his emotional intensity and he considered that daughters needed special affection. He was our father, friend, role model and teacher, and he was our reference in all our personal matters, so we didn't do anything until we consulted him. We had reassurance for all his decisions. His presence made us feel strong and safe and he was careful not to make us feel the difficulty of security life, but to make it as easy and enjoyable as possible. He has given us enough affection during his life to last us for years.

Q: What were the most important priorities of the martyr Zulfikar in raising his children and what was his greatest influence on you as a family?

He has always been repeating and working on implanting some basic concepts in us, including:

First, the religious aspect: He emphasized the subject of proper prayer since childhood, when he attended, he watched how we performed the pillars of prayer, and he was interested in the legitimate dress and emphasized it. 

Secondly, on the family side and family duties: He stressed the importance of the relationship of the family and made sure that everyone attended family meetings even if he was not there, as well as canceled everything else. He wanted us to realize the importance of this topic from a religious and social point of view, to ask and reassure about relatives and their children because he believed that the West is working to break up the family and we should realize the importance of this topic, and its seriousness, and install these correct Islamic customs.

Third, on the scientific side: Everyone who knows the master knows his reverence for science, he wanted us to learn and continue our studies. During the official certificate exams, when we returned, he would review the exam question paper with us and discuss the answers with us, even when I joined the University, he followed up with me to record the materials. He was very interested in this topic.

"We are in front of an advanced and educated enemy and we have to face it in the same way but surpass it," he was the author of the idea of an educated Mujahid in the ranks of Hezbollah.

I used to joke with him sometimes and say to him: "Will I study all these years so as not to take money in return?" He told me: "Not every work needs a return, there is the concept of giving and service, and there's something greater than this work", and I think this is a very deep concept. 

Fourth, the concept of Man: I do not forget my father's phrase in every session since childhood, "A person should not come to this world without leaving a mark, a mark in which he serves Islam in any field, if he does not want to leave a mark and play the role of Allah's successor on Earth, then what is his job?"

Coinciding with the nationalization day of the oil industry, the first part of phase 2 of the Abadan Refinery capacity development and stabilization plan, which is considered one of the largest refining projects in the West Asia region, was put into operation with the presence of the president.

Output in the refinery, which is Iran’s largest and oldest, will reach to cover 14 million liters per day of gasoline and 20 million liters per day of diesel.

Oil Ministry authorities said the expansion project in Abadan Refinery is aimed at reducing Iran’s vulnerability to sanctions imposed on its direct oil exports and to complete the value chain in the country’s oil sector.  

The project will also boost the quality of the fuels produced in Abadan Refinery and will lead to a reduction in emissions by cutting back on mazut production in the facility from 45% to some 26%.
